speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people call you the godmother of AI.
zh: 很多人称呼你为 AI 教母。

speaker: Unknown
en: The work you did actually was the spark that brought us out of AI winter.
zh: 你所做的工作实际上是火花，带我们走出了 AI 寒冬。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the middle of 2015, middle of 2016, some tech companies avoid using the word AI because they were not sure if AI was a dirty word.
zh: 在 2015 年年中，2016 年年中，一些科技公司避免使用 AI 这个词，因为他们不确定 AI 是不是一个脏词。

speaker: Unknown
en: 2017-ish was the beginning of companies calling themselves AI companies.
zh: 2017 年左右是公司自称 AI 公司的开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this line, I think, this was when you were presenting to Congress.
zh: 有这句话，我想，这是你在向国会做陈述的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nothing artificial about AI.
zh: AI 没有任何人工之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's inspired by people.
zh: 它的灵感来源于人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's created by people, and most importantly, it impacts people.
zh: 它由人创造，最重要的是，它影响着人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not like I think AI will have no impact on jobs or people.
zh: 并不是我认为 AI 会对工作或人没有影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, I believe that whatever AI does, currently or in the future, is up to us.
zh: 事实上，我相信无论 AI 做什么，目前还是未来，都取决于我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's up to the people.
zh: 取决于人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do believe technology is a net positive for humanity, but I think every technology is a double-edged sword.
zh: 我确实相信技术对人类来说是净积极的，但我认为每项技术都是一把双刃剑。

speaker: Unknown
en: If we're not doing the right thing as a society, as individuals, we can screw this up as well.
zh: 如果我们作为社会，作为个人，不做正确的事情，我们也可能搞砸它。

speaker: Unknown
en: You had this breakthrough insight of just, okay, we can train machines to think like humans, but it's just missing the data that humans have to learn as a child.
zh: 你有这个突破性的洞察，好吧，我们可以训练机器像人类一样思考，但它只是缺少人类在孩童时期必须学习的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: I chose to look at artificial intelligence through the lens of visual intelligence because humans are deeply visual animals.
zh: 我选择通过视觉智能的视角来看待人工智能，因为人类是深度视觉动物。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to train machines with as much information as possible on images of objects, but objects are very, very difficult to learn.
zh: 我们需要用尽可能多的关于物体图像的信息来训练机器，但是物体非常非常难学。

speaker: Unknown
en: A single object can have infinite possibilities that is shown on an image.
zh: 单个物体在图像上可能显示出无限的可能性。

speaker: Unknown
en: In order to train computers with tens and thousands of object concepts, you really need to show it millions of examples.
zh: 为了用数以万计的物体概念训练计算机，你真的需要给它展示数百万个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Dr. Fei-Fei Li, who's known as the godmother of AI.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Fei-Fei Li 博士，她被称为 AI 教母。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fei-Fei has been responsible for and at the center of many of the biggest breakthroughs that sparked the AI revolution that we're currently living through.
zh: Fei-Fei 负责并处于许多最大突破的中心，这些突破引发了我们目前正在经历的 AI 革命。

speaker: Unknown
en: She spearheaded the creation of ImageNet, which was basically her realizing that AI needed a ton of clean-labeled data to get smarter, and that data set became the breakthrough that led to the current approach to building and scaling AI models.
zh: 她主导了 ImageNet 的创建，这基本上是她意识到 AI 需要大量干净标记的数据才能变得更聪明，而且那个数据集成为了突破，导致了当前构建和扩展 AI 模型的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was chief AI scientist at Google Cloud, which is where some of the biggest early technology breakthroughs emerged from.
zh: 她曾是谷歌云的首席 AI 科学家，这也是一些最大的早期技术突破涌现的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was director at SAIL, Stanford's Artificial Intelligence Lab, where many of the biggest AI minds came out of.
zh: 她曾是 SAIL 的主管，即斯坦福人工智能实验室，那里诞生了许多最伟大的 AI 思想。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's also co-creator of Stanford's Human-Centered AI Institute, which is playing a vital role in a direction that AI is taking.
zh: 她也是斯坦福以人为中心的人工智能研究所的联合创始人，该研究所正在 AI 的发展方向上发挥着至关重要的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's also been on the board of Twitter.
zh: 她也曾在 Twitter 董事会任职。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was named one of Time's 100 Most Influential People in AI.
zh: 她被命名为时代杂志 AI 领域 100 位最具影响力人物之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's also United Nations advisory board.
zh: 她也是联合国咨询委员会成员。

speaker: Unknown
en: I could go on.
zh: 我可以继续说下去。

speaker: Unknown
en: In our conversation, Fei-Fei shares a brief history of how we got to today in the world of AI, including this mind-blowing reminder that 9 to 10 years ago, calling yourself an AI company was basically a death knell for your brand because no one believed that AI was actually going to work.
zh: 在我们的对话中，Fei-Fei 分享了我们如何在 AI 世界走到今天的简短历史，包括这个令人震惊的提醒，9 到 10 年前，自称 AI 公司基本上对你品牌的丧钟，因为没有人相信 AI 真的会奏效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, it's completely different.
zh: 今天，完全不同了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every company is an AI company.
zh: 每家公司都是 AI 公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: We also chat about her take on how she sees AI impacting humanity in the future, how far current technologies will take us, why she's so passionate about building a world model and what exactly world models are, and most exciting of all, the launch of the world's first large world model, Marble, which just came out as this podcast comes out.
zh: 我们还聊到了她对 AI 未来如何影响人类的看法，当前技术能带我们走多远，为什么她对构建世界模型如此充满热情，以及世界模型究竟是什么，最令人兴奋的是，世界上第一个大型世界模型 Marble 的发布，就在这个播客发布时刚刚推出。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyone can go play with this at marble.worldlabs.ai.
zh: 任何人都可以去 marble.worldlabs.ai 玩一玩。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's insane.
zh: 这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Definitely check it out.
zh: 一定要去看看。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fei-Fei is incredible and way too under the radar for the impact that she's had on the world, so I am really excited to have her on and to spread her wisdom with more people.
zh: Fei-Fei 令人难以置信，考虑到她对世界的影响力，她太低调了，所以我真的很高兴邀请她来，并将她的智慧传播给更多的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: A huge thank you to Ben Horowitz and Condoleezza Rice for suggesting topics for this conversation.
zh: 非常感谢 Ben Horowitz 和 Condoleezza Rice 为本次对话建议话题。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了订阅并在你最喜欢的播客应用或 YouTube 上关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Dr. Fei-Fei Li after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 有了这个，在简短的赞助商广告之后，我为你带来 Fei-Fei Li 博士。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Figma, makers of Figma Make.
zh: 这一集由 Figma 带给你，Figma Make 的制造商。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I was a PM at Airbnb, I still remember when Figma came out and how much it improved how we operated as a team.
zh: 当我在 Airbnb 做 PM 时，我仍然记得 Figma 出来的时候，它极大地提高了我们作为一个团队的运作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Suddenly, I can involve my whole team in the design process, give feedback on design concepts really quickly and it just made the whole product development process so much more fun.
zh: 突然间，我可以让我的整个团队参与设计过程，非常快地对设计概念给出反馈，这让整个产品开发过程变得有趣多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But Figma never felt like it was for me.
zh: 但 Figma 感觉从来都不是为我准备的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was great for giving feedback and designs, but as a builder, I wanted to make stuff.
zh: 它非常适合给出反馈和设计，但作为一个构建者，我想制作东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why Figma built Figma Make.
zh: 这就是为什么 Figma 构建了 Figma Make。

speaker: Unknown
en: With just a few prompts, you can make any idea or design into a fully functional prototype or app that anyone can iterate on and validate with customers.
zh: 只需几个提示，你就可以把任何想法或设计变成一个完全可用的原型或应用程序，任何人都可以对其进行迭代并与客户验证。

speaker: Unknown
en: Figma Make is a different kind of vibe coding tool.
zh: Figma Make 是一种不同类型的 vibe coding 工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because it's all in Figma, you can use your team's existing design building blocks, making it easy to create outputs that look good and feel real and are connected to how your team builds.
zh: 因为它都在 Figma 中，你可以使用团队现有的设计构建块，使得创建看起来很好、感觉很真实并且与团队构建方式相连的输出变得容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stop spending so much time telling people about your product vision and instead show it to them.
zh: 别花那么多时间告诉人们你的产品愿景，而是展示给他们看。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make code-back prototypes and apps fast with Figma Make.
zh: 使用 Figma Make 快速制作代码支持的原型和应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check it out at figma.com/lenny.
zh: 在 figma.com/lenny 看看吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did you know that I have a whole team that helps me with my podcast and with my newsletter?
zh: 你知道我有一整个团队帮我做播客和newsletter吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I want everyone on that team to be super happy and thrive in the roles.
zh: 我希望那个团队里的每个人都在他们的角色中超级快乐和蓬勃发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: Justworks knows that your employees are more than just your employees; they're your people.
zh: Justworks 知道你的员工不仅仅是你的员工；他们是你的“人”。

speaker: Unknown
en: My team is spread out across Colorado, Australia, Nepal, West Africa, and San Francisco.
zh: 我的团队分布在科罗拉多、澳大利亚、尼泊尔、西非和旧金山。

speaker: Unknown
en: My life would be so incredibly complicated to hire people internationally, to pay people on time and in their local currencies, and to answer their HR questions 24/7.
zh: 在国际上招聘人、按时用当地货币支付工资、以及全天候回答他们的人力资源问题，我的生活将会变得极其复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: But with Justworks, it's super easy.
zh: 但有了 Justworks，这超级简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you're setting up your own automated payroll, offering premium benefits, or hiring internationally, Justworks offer simple software and 24/7 human support from small business experts for you and your people.
zh: 无论你是设置自己的自动薪资单、提供高级福利，还是在国际上招聘，Justworks 为你和你的“人”提供简单的软件和来自小企业专家的 24/7 人工支持。

speaker: Unknown
en: They do your human resources right so that you can do right by your people.
zh: 他们帮你搞定人力资源，这样你就能善待你的“人”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Justworks, for your people.
zh: Justworks，为了你的“人”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fei-Fei, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Fei-Fei，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎参加播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to be here, Lenny.
zh: 我很高兴来到这里，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm even more excited to have you here.
zh: 我更高兴你能来这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is such a treat to get to chat with you.
zh: 能和你聊天真是太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much that I want to talk about.
zh: 我有很多想谈论的话题。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've been at the center of this AI explosion that we're seeing right now for so long.
zh: 我们现在看到的这场 AI 爆炸，你已经处于中心很长时间了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to talk about a bunch of the history that I think a lot of people don't even know about how this whole thing started, but let me first read a quote from Wired about you just so people get a sense, and in the intro I'll share all of the other epic things you've done.
zh: 我们将谈论很多历史，我想很多人甚至不知道这一切是如何开始的，但首先让我读一段《连线》杂志关于你的引述，以便人们了解一下，并且在前言中我会分享你做过的所有其他史诗级的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think this is a good way to just set context.
zh: 但我认为这是一个设定背景的好方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Fei-Fei is one of a tiny group of scientists, a group perhaps small enough to fit around a kitchen table, who are responsible for AI's recent remarkable advances."
zh: “Fei-Fei 是一小群科学家中的一员，这群人也许少到可以围坐在厨房餐桌旁，他们对 AI 最近的显著进步负有责任。”

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people call you the godmother of AI, and unlike a lot of AI leaders, you're an AI optimist.
zh: 很多人称你为 AI 的教母，而且与许多 AI 领导者不同，你是一个 AI 乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't think AI is going to replace us.
zh: 你不认为 AI 会取代我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't think it's going to take all our jobs.
zh: 你不认为它会抢走我们所有的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't think it's going to kill us.
zh: 你不认为它会杀死我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I thought it'd be fun to start there, just what's your perspective on how AI is going to impact humanity over time?
zh: 所以我想从那里开始会很有趣，你对 AI 将如何影响人类有什么看法？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, okay, so Lenny, let me be very clear.
zh: 是的，好的，Lenny，让我说清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not a utopian, so it's not like I think AI will have no impact on jobs or people.
zh: 我不是一个乌托邦主义者，所以并不是我认为 AI 对工作或人没有影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, I'm a humanist.
zh: 事实上，我是一个人文主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe that whatever AI does, currently or in the future, is up to us.
zh: 我相信无论 AI 做什么，现在或未来，都取决于我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's up to the people.
zh: 这取决于人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I do believe technology is a net positive for humanity.
zh: 所以我确实相信技术对人类来说是净收益。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look at the long course of civilization, I think we are, and fundamentally, we're an innovative species that we... If you look at from written record thousands of years ago to now, humans just kept innovating ourselves and innovating our tools, and with that, we make lives better, we make work better, we build civilization, and I do believe AI is part of that.
zh: 如果你放眼文明的漫长历程，我认为我们是，从根本上说，我们是一个创新的物种，我们……如果你看从几千年前有文字记录到现在，人类只是在不断创新自己和不断创新我们的工具，随之而来的是，我们让生活更美好，我们让工作更好，我们建立文明，我确实相信 AI 是其中的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's where the optimism comes from.
zh: 这就是乐观主义的来源。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think every technology is a double-edged sword, and if we're not doing the right thing as a species, as a society, as communities, as individuals, we can screw this up as well.
zh: 但我认为每一种技术都是一把双刃剑，如果我们作为一个物种、一个社会、社区、个人不做正确的事情，我们也可能搞砸它。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this line, I think, this was when you were presenting to Congress, "There's nothing artificial about AI.
zh: 有这句话，我想，这是当你向国会作证时说的，“AI 没有任何人工的成分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's inspired by people.
zh: 它的灵感来自人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's created by people, and most importantly, it impacts people."
zh: 它是由人创造的，最重要的是，它影响人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have a question there, but what a great line.
zh: 我对此没有问题，但这真是一句很棒的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I feel pretty deeply.
zh: 是的，我感触很深。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started working AI two and a half decades ago, and I've been having students for the past two decades and almost every student who graduates, I remind them when they graduate from my lab that your field is called artificial intelligence, but there's nothing artificial about it.
zh: 我在两个半世纪前开始研究 AI，在过去二十年里我几乎每个学生毕业时，我都在他们毕业时提醒他们，你的领域叫人工智能，但其中没有任何人工的成分。

speaker: Unknown
en: Coming back to the point you just made about how it's kind of up to us about where this all goes, what is it you think we need to get right?
zh: 回到你刚才提到的关于这一切走向取决于我们要点的观点，你认为我们需要做对的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we set things on a path?
zh: 我们如何让事情步入正轨？

speaker: Unknown
en: I know this is a very difficult question to answer, but just what's your advice?
zh: 我知道这是一个很难回答的问题，但你的建议是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you think we should be keeping in mind?
zh: 你认为我们应该记住什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, how many hours do we have?
zh: 是的，我们要在这个问题上花多少小时？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we align AI?
zh: 我们如何让 AI 与人类价值观对齐？

speaker: Unknown
en: There we go.
zh: 我们开始吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's solve it.
zh: 让我们解决这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think people should be responsible individuals no matter what we do.
zh: 所以我认为人们无论做什么都应该是负责任的个体。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what we teach our children, and this is what we need to do as grownups as well.
zh: 这是我们要教给孩子的，这也是我们作为成年人需要做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: No matter which part of the AI development or AI deployment or AI application you are participating in, and most likely many of us, especially as technologists, we're in multiple points.
zh: 无论你参与 AI 开发、AI 部署还是 AI 应用的哪个部分，而且很可能我们很多人，尤其是作为技术人员，都处于多个环节。

speaker: Unknown
en: We should act like responsible individuals and care about this.
zh: 我们应该像负责任的个人一样行事，并关心这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Actually, care a lot about this.
zh: 事实上，非常关心这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think everybody today should care about AI because it is going to impact your individual life.
zh: 我认为今天每个人都应该关心人工智能，因为它将影响你的个人生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is going to impact your community, it's going to impact the society and the future generation.
zh: 它将影响你的社区，它将影响社会和后代。

speaker: Unknown
en: And caring about it as a responsible person is the first, but also the most important step.
zh: 作为一个负责任的人关心它是第一步，也是最重要的一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so let me actually take a step back and kind of go to the beginning of AI.
zh: 好的，所以让我实际上退后一步，回到人工智能的开端。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people started hearing and caring about AI, as what it's called today, just like, I don't know, a few years ago when ChatGPT came out.
zh: 大多数人开始听说并关心人工智能，也就是现在所谓的，就在，我不知道，几年前当 ChatGPT 出来的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe it was like three years ago.
zh: 可能大约是三年前。

speaker: Unknown
en: Three years ago, almost one more month, three years ago.
zh: 三年前，再过差不多一个月就三年前了。

speaker: Lenny
en: Wow, okay. And that was ChatGPT coming out. Is that the milestone you have in mind?
zh: 哇，好的。那是 ChatGPT 的发布。那是你心目中的里程碑吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, cool.
zh: 好的，酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's exactly how I saw it.
zh: 这正是我所看到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But very few people know there was a long, long history of people working on, it was called machine learning back then and there's other terms, and now it's just everything's AI and there was kind of a long period of just a lot of people working on it.
zh: 但很少有人知道有很长的历史，人们在致力于它，那时被称为机器学习还有其他术语，现在一切都是人工智能，并且有一段很长的时间只是很多人在致力于它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's this what people refer to as the AI winter where people just gave up almost, most people did, and just, okay, this idea isn't going anywhere.
zh: 然后就是人们所谓的 AI 寒冬，那时人们几乎放弃了，大多数人确实放弃了，只是，好吧，这个想法不会有什么结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the work you did actually was essentially the spark that brought us out of AI winter and is directly responsible for the world where now of just AI is all we talk about.
zh: 然后你做的工作实际上本质上就是火花，将我们从 AI 寒冬中带了出来，并且直接对现在的世界负责，现在我们只谈论人工智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you just said, it's going to impact everything we do.
zh: 正如你刚才所说，它将影响我们要做的每一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I thought it'd be really interesting to hear from you just the brief history of what the world was like before ImageNet and just the work you did to create ImageNet, why that was so important, and then just what happened after.
zh: 所以我认为听听你的简短历史会非常有趣，关于 ImageNet 之前的世界是什么样的，以及你为创建 ImageNet 所做的工作，为什么那如此重要，以及之后发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is, for me, hard to keep in mind that AI is so new for everybody when I lived my entire professional life in AI.
zh: 对我来说，很难记住人工智能对每个人来说都是如此之新，因为我的整个职业生涯都在人工智能领域度过。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a part of me that is just, it's so satisfying to see a personal curiosity that I started barely out of teenagehood and now has become a transformative force of our civilization.
zh: 我的一部分只是，看到我在刚出青少年时期开始的个人好奇心，现在已经变成了我们文明的一种变革力量，这是非常令人满足的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It generally is a civilizational level technology.
zh: 它通常是一种文明水平的技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that journey is about 30 years or 20 something, 20 plus years, and it's just very satisfying.
zh: 所以那段旅程大约是 30 年或 20 多年，20 多年，并且非常令人满足。

speaker: Unknown
en: So where did it all start?
zh: 那么这一切是从哪里开始的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I'm not even the first generation AI researcher.
zh: 嗯，我甚至不是第一代人工智能研究员。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first generation really date back to the '50s and '60s, and Alan Turing was ahead of his time in the '40s by asking, daring humanity with the question, "Is there thinking machines?"
zh: 第一代真正追溯到 50 年代和 60 年代，艾伦·图灵在 40 年代超越了他的时代，通过这个问题大胆挑战人类，“有思维机器吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course he has a specific way of testing this concept of thinking machine, which is a conversational chatbot, which to his standard we now have a thinking machine.
zh: 当然他有一种特定的方式来测试思维机器的概念，这是一个对话聊天机器人，按照他的标准我们现在有了思维机器。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that was just a more anecdotal inspiration.
zh: 但那只是一个更多轶事的灵感。

speaker: Unknown
en: The field really began in the '50s when computer scientists came together and look at how we can use computer programs and algorithms to build these programs that can do things that have been only capable by human cognition.
zh: 该领域真正开始于 50 年代，当时计算机科学家聚集在一起，看看我们如何使用计算机程序和算法来构建这些只能由人类认知完成的程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that was the beginning.
zh: 那就是开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the founding fathers the Dartmouth workshop in the 1956, we have Professor John McCarthy who later came to Stanford who coined the term artificial intelligence.
zh: 创始人们，1956 年的达特茅斯研讨会，我们有 John McCarthy 教授，他后来来到斯坦福，创造了人工智能这个术语。

speaker: Unknown
en: And between the '50s, '60s, '70s, and '80s, it was the early days of AI exploration and we had logic systems, we had expert systems, we also had early exploration of neural network.
zh: 在 50 年代、60 年代、70 年代和 80 年代之间，这是人工智能探索的早期日子，我们有逻辑系统，我们有专家系统，我们也有神经网络的早期探索。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it came to around the late '80s, the '90s, and the very beginning of the 21st century.
zh: 然后到了 80 年代末、90 年代和 21 世纪初。

speaker: Unknown
en: That stretch about 20 years is actually the beginning of machine learning, is the marriage between computer programming and statistical learning.
zh: 这段大约 20 年的时间实际上是机器学习的开始，是计算机编程和统计学习的结合。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that marriage brought a very, very critical concept into AI, which is that purely rule-based program is not going to account for the vast amount of cognitive capabilities that we imagine computers can do.
zh: 这种结合给人工智能带来一个非常关键的概念，那就是纯粹的基于规则的程序无法解释我们想象计算机可以做的海量认知能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have to use machines to learn the patterns.
zh: 所以我们必须使用机器来学习模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once the machines can learn the patterns, it has a hope to do more things.
zh: 一旦机器可以学习模式，它就有希望做更多的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, if you give it three cats, the hope is not just for the machines to recognize these three cats.
zh: 例如，如果你给它三只猫，希望不仅仅是机器识别这三只猫。

speaker: Unknown
en: The hope is the machines can recognize the fourth cat, the fifth cat, the sixth cat, and all the other cats.
zh: 希望是机器可以识别第四只猫，第五只猫，第六只猫，以及所有其他的猫。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a learning ability that is fundamental to humans and remaining animals.
zh: 这是一种对人类和剩余动物来说根本的学习能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we, as a field, realized, "We need machine learning."
zh: 我们，作为一个领域，意识到，“我们需要机器学习。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was up till the beginning of the 21st century.
zh: 所以那一直持续到 21 世纪初。

speaker: Unknown
en: I entered the field of AI literally in the year of 2000.
zh: 我在 2000 年进入了人工智能领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's when my PhD began at Caltech.
zh: 那是我在加州理工学院开始博士学位的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I was one of the first generation machine learning researchers and we were already studying this concept of machine learning, especially neural network.
zh: 所以我是第一批机器学习研究人员之一，我们已经在研究机器学习的概念，特别是神经网络。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember that was one of my first courses at Caltech is called neural network, but it was very painful.
zh: 我记得那是我在加州理工学院的第一门课程之一叫神经网络，但这非常痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was still smack in the middle of the so-called AI winter, meaning the public didn't look at this too much.
zh: 它仍然 smack 在所谓的 AI 寒冬的中间，意味着公众对此关注不多。

speaker: Unknown
en: There wasn't that much funding, but there was also a lot of ideas flowing around.
zh: 没有那么多资金，但也有很多想法在流传。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think two things happened to myself that brought my own career so close to the birth of modern AI is that I chose to look at artificial intelligence through the lens of visual intelligence because humans are deeply visual animals.
zh: 我认为我自己发生了两件事，使我自己的职业生涯如此接近现代人工智能的诞生，是我选择通过视觉智能的视角来看待人工智能，因为人类是深深依赖视觉的动物。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can talk a little more later, but so much of our intelligence is built upon visual, perceptual, spatial understanding, not just language per se.
zh: 我们可以稍后多谈谈，但我们的很多智能是建立在视觉、感知、空间理解之上的，而不仅仅是语言本身。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think they're complementary.
zh: 我认为它们是互补的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I choose to look at visual intelligence and my PhD and my early professor years, my students and I are very committed to a north star problem, which is solving the problem of object recognition because it's a building block for the perceptual world, right?
zh: 所以我选择研究视觉智能，在我的博士生涯和早期教授岁月里，我和我的学生非常致力于解决一个北极星问题，即解决物体识别的问题，因为它是感知世界的构建模块，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: We go around the world interpreting reasoning and interacting with it more or less at the object level.
zh: 我们在世界各地漫游，解释、推理并以某种方式与世界互动，这基本上是在物体层面上进行的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't interact with the world at the molecular level.
zh: 我们不在分子层面上与世界互动。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't interact with the world as... We sometimes do, but we rarely, for example, if you want to lift a teapot, you don't say, "Okay, the teapot is made of a hundred pieces of porcelain and let me work on this a hundred pieces."
zh: 我们不在……层面上与世界互动。我们有时会，但很少，例如，如果你想拿起一个茶壶，你不会说，“好吧，这个茶壶是由一百块瓷器组成的，让我来处理这一百块。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You look at this as one object and interact with it.
zh: 你把它看作一个物体并与它互动。

speaker: Unknown
en: So object is really important.
zh: 所以物体真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I was among the first researchers to identify this as a north star problem, but I think what happened is that as a student of AI and a researcher of AI, I was working on all kinds of mathematical models including neural network, including Bayesian network, including many, many models.
zh: 所以我是第一批将此确定为北极星问题的研究人员之一，但我认为发生的事情是，作为一名 AI 的学生和研究人员，我研究了各种数学模型，包括神经网络，包括贝叶斯网络，包括非常非常多的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there was one singular pain point is that these models don't have data to be trained on.
zh: 而且有一个特别的痛点是，这些模型没有数据可供训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a field, we were so focusing on these models, but it dawned on me that human learning as well as evolution is actually a big data learning process.
zh: 作为一个领域，我们当时如此专注于这些模型，但我突然意识到，人类的学习以及进化实际上是一个大数据学习过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Humans learn with so much experience constantly.
zh: 人类在如此多的经验中不断学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the evolution, if you look at time, animals evolve with just experiencing the world.
zh: 在进化中，如果你看时间线，动物通过仅仅体验世界而进化。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think my students and I conjectured that a very critically-overlooked ingredient of bringing AI to life is big data.
zh: 所以我认为我和我的学生推测，将 AI 带到生命的一个非常关键被忽视的要素是大数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we began this ImageNet project in 2006, 2007.
zh: 然后我们在 2006 年、2007 年开始了这个 ImageNet 项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were very ambitious.
zh: 我们非常有野心。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want to get the entire internet's image data on objects.
zh: 我们想要获取整个互联网关于物体的图像数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now granted internet was a lot smaller than today, so I felt like that ambition was at least not too crazy.
zh: 现在承认当时的互联网比今天小得多，所以我觉得这种野心至少不太疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, it's totally delusional to think a couple of graduate student and a professor can do this.
zh: 现在，认为几个研究生和一个教授能做到这一点是完全妄想。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what we did.
zh: 而这就是我们所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We curated very carefully, 15 million images on the internet, created a taxonomy of 22,000 concepts, borrowing other researchers' work like linguists work on WordNet, and it's a particular way of dictionarying words.
zh: 我们非常小心地策划了互联网上的 1500 万张图像，创建了包含 22,000 个概念的分类法，借用了其他研究人员的工作，比如语言学家在 WordNet 上的工作，这是一种特定的词典编纂方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we combine that into ImageNet and we open-sourced that to the research community.
zh: 我们将其组合成 ImageNet 并将其开源给研究界。

speaker: Unknown
en: We held an annual ImageNet challenge to encourage everybody to participate in this.
zh: 我们举办了一年一度的 ImageNet 挑战赛来鼓励每个人参与其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: We continue to do our own research, but 2012 was the moment that many people think was the beginning of the deep learning or birth of modern AI because a group of Toronto researchers led by Professor Geoff Hinton, participated in ImageNet Challenge, used ImageNet big data and two GPUs from NVIDIA and created successfully the first neural network algorithm that can...
zh: 我们继续做我们自己的研究，但 2012 年是许多人认为深度学习开始或现代 AI 诞生的时刻，因为由 Geoff Hinton 教授领导的一组多伦多研究人员，参加了 ImageNet 挑战赛，使用了 ImageNet 大数据和来自 NVIDIA 的两张 GPU，成功创建了第一个能够……的神经网络算法

speaker: Unknown
en: It didn't totally solve, but made a huge progress towards solving the problem of object recognition.
zh: 它没有完全解决，但在解决物体识别问题上取得了巨大进展。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that combination of the trio technology, big data, neural network, and GPU was kind of the golden recipe for modern AI.
zh: 这种三重技术的结合，大数据、神经网络和 GPU，有点像是现代 AI 的黄金配方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then fast-forward, the public moment of AI, which is the ChatGPT moment, if you look at the ingredients of what brought ChatGPT to the world technically still use these three ingredients.
zh: 然后快进到 AI 的公众时刻，也就是 ChatGPT 时刻，如果你看看将 ChatGPT 带到世界的要素，技术上仍然使用这三个要素。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, it's internet-scale data mostly texts is a much more complex neural network architecture than 2012, but it's still neural network and a lot more GPUs, but it's still GPUs.
zh: 现在，主要是文本的互联网规模数据是一种比 2012 年复杂得多的神经网络架构，但它仍然是神经网络，以及更多的 GPU，但它仍然是 GPU。

speaker: Unknown
en: So these three ingredients are still at the core of modern AI.
zh: 所以这三个要素仍然是现代 AI 的核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Incredible.
zh: 难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have never heard that full story before.
zh: 我以前从未听过那个完整的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that it was two GPUs was the first.
zh: 我很喜欢它是两张 GPU 起家的这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that.
zh: 我很喜欢那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now it's, I don't know, hundreds of thousands, right, that are orders of magnitude more powerful.
zh: 而现在是，我不知道，成千上万，对吧，那是数量级上更强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yep.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those two GPUs where they just bought, they were like gaming GPUs, they just went to the-
zh: 那两张 GPU 只是买来的，它们就像游戏 GPU，他们只是去……

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... GameStar that people use for playing games.
zh: ……人们用来玩游戏的 GameStar。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, this continues to be in a large way, the way models get smarter.
zh: 正如你所说，这在很大程度上仍然是模型变得更聪明的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of the fastest growing companies in the world right now, I've had them all mostly on the podcast, Mercor and Surge and Scale.
zh: 目前世界上一些增长最快的公司，我大多都请过上播客，Mercor、Surge 和 Scale。

speaker: Unknown
en: They continue to do this for labs, just give them more and more label data of the things they're most excited and interested in.
zh: 它们继续为实验室做这件事，只是给他们越来越多他们最兴奋和感兴趣事物的标签数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I remember Alex Wang from Scale very early days.
zh: 是的，我记得 Scale 的 Alex Wang 在很早期的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: I probably still has his emails when he was starting Scale.
zh: 我可能还保留着他刚开始做 Scale 时的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: He was very kind.
zh: 他非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: He keeps sending me emails about how image that inspired Scale.
zh: 他一直给我发邮件，讲述那个启发了 Scale 的图像。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was very pleased to see that.
zh: 我很高兴看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of my other favorite takeaways from what you just shared is just such an example of high agency and just doing things that's kind of a meme on Twitter.
zh: 我从你刚才分享的内容中得到的另一个最喜欢的启示，就是这样一个高度主观能动性的例子，就像 Twitter 上的一个梗。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just you can just do things.
zh: 就是你就可以做事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're just like, okay, this is probably necessary to move AI.
zh: 你就像，好吧，这对于推动 AI 可能是必要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's called machine learning back then, right?
zh: 那时候它叫机器学习，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Was that the term most people used?
zh: 那是大多数人使用的术语吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it was interchangeably.
zh: 我认为它们是可以互换使用的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's true.
zh: 这是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do remember the companies, the tech companies, I am not going to name names, but I was in a conversation in one of the early days, I think is in the middle of 2015, middle of 2016, some tech companies avoid using the word AI because they were not sure if AI was a dirty word.
zh: 我确实记得那些公司，那些科技公司，我不打算指名道姓，但在早期的一次对话中，我想是在 2015 年中期，2016 年中期，有些科技公司避免使用 AI 这个词，因为它们不确定 AI 是否是一个贬义词。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember I was actually encouraging everybody to use the word AI because to me that is one of the most audacious question humanity has ever asked in our quest for science and technology, and I feel very proud of this term.
zh: 我记得我当时实际上是在鼓励每个人都使用 AI 这个词，因为对我来说，这是人类在科学技术探索中提出的最大胆的问题之一，我为这个术语感到非常自豪。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yes, at the beginning some people were not sure.
zh: 但是的，起初有些人并不确定。

speaker: Unknown
en: What year was that roughly when AI was a dirty word?
zh: AI 大约是在哪一年成为贬义词的？

speaker: Unknown
en: 2016, I think because that was-
zh: 我想是 2016 年，因为那是——

speaker: Unknown
en: 2016, less than 10 years ago.

speaker: Unknown
en: That was the changing.
zh: 那是一个转折点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some people start calling it AI, but I think if you look at the Silicon Valley tech companies, if you trace their marketing term, I think 2017-ish was the beginning of companies calling themselves AI companies.
zh: 有些人开始称其为 AI，但我想如果你看看硅谷的科技公司，如果你追溯他们的营销术语，我想 2017 年左右是公司自称 AI 公司的开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's incredible.
zh: 这太不可思议了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just how the world has changed.
zh: 世界变化得真大。

speaker: Dr. Fei-Fei Li
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, you can't not call yourself an AI company.
zh: 现在，你不能不称自己为 AI 公司。

speaker: Dr. Fei-Fei Li
en: I know.
zh: 我知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just nine-ish years later.
zh: 就在大约九年后。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else around the history, that early history that you think people don't know that you think is important before we chat about where you think things are going and the work that you're doing?
zh: 关于历史，那段早期历史，还有什么你觉得大家不知道但你认为很重要的事情，在我们讨论你认为事情的发展方向以及你正在做的工作之前吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think as all histories, I'm keenly aware that I am recognized for being part of the history, but there are so many heroes and so many researchers.
zh: 我想正如所有的历史一样，我敏锐地意识到我因作为历史的一部分而被认可，但也有太多的英雄和太多的研究人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're talking about generations of researchers.
zh: 我们谈论的是几代研究人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: In my own world, there are so many people who have inspired me, which I talked about in my book, but I do feel our culture, especially Silicon Valley, tends to assign achievements to a single person.
zh: 在我自己的世界里，有很多人启发了我，我在书中谈到了他们，但我确实觉得我们的文化，尤其是硅谷，倾向于将成就归于一个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: While I think it has value, but it's just to be remembered.
zh: 虽然我认为这有价值，但这只是需要被记住。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is a field of, at this point, 70 years old and we have gone through many generations.
zh: AI 是一个迄今已有 70 年历史的领域，我们已经经历了几代人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody, no one could have gotten here by themselves.
zh: 没有人，没有人能够独自走到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so let me ask you this question.
zh: 好的，那我问你这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like we're always on this precipice of AGI, this kind of vague term people throw around, AGI is coming, it's going to take over everything.
zh: 感觉我们总是处于 AGI 的边缘，人们随意抛出的这个模糊术语，AGI 即将来临，它将接管一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your take on how far you think we might be from AGI?
zh: 对于你认为我们距离 AGI 还有多远，你有什么看法？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we're going to get there on the current trajectory we're on?
zh: 你认为我们会在当前的轨迹上实现它吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we need more breakthroughs?
zh: 你认为我们需要更多的突破吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think the current approach will get us there?
zh: 你认为当前的方法能让我们实现它吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is a very interesting term, Lenny.
zh: 是的，Lenny，这是一个非常有趣的术语。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if anyone has ever defined AGI.
zh: 我不知道是否有人定义过 AGI。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are many different definitions, including some kind of superpower for machines all the way to machines can become economically viable agent in the society.
zh: 有许多不同的定义，包括某种机器的超能力，一直到机器可以在社会中成为经济上可行的智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: In other words, making salaries to live.
zh: 换句话说，赚取薪水来维持生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the definition of AGI?
zh: 这是 AGI 的定义吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: As a scientist, I take science very seriously and I enter the field because I was inspired by this audacious question of, can machines think and do things in the way that humans can do?
zh: 作为一名科学家，我非常认真地对待科学，我进入这个领域是因为我受到了这个大胆问题的启发，机器能思考并以人类能做的方式做事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: For me, that's always the north star of AI.
zh: 对我来说，这始终是 AI 的北极星。

speaker: Unknown
en: And from that point of view, I don't know what's the difference between AI and AGI.
zh: 从那个角度来看，我不知道 AI 和 AGI 之间有什么区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we've done very well in achieving parts of the goal, including conversational AI, but I don't think we have completely conquered all the goals of AI.
zh: 我认为我们在实现部分目标方面做得很好，包括对话式 AI，但我不认为我们已经完全征服了 AI 的所有目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think our founding fathers, Alan Turing, I wonder if Alan Turing is around today and you ask him to contrast AI versus AGI, he might just shrugged and said, "Well, I asked the same question back in 1940s," so I don't want to get onto a rabbit hole of defining AI versus AGI.
zh: 我认为我们的奠基人，Alan Turing，我想知道如果 Alan Turing 今天在世，你让他对比 AI 与 AGI，他可能只是耸耸肩说，“好吧，我在 1940 年代就问过同样的问题”，所以我不想陷入定义 AI 与 AGI 的兔子洞。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel AGI is more a marketing term than a scientific term as a scientist than technologist.
zh: 我觉得 AGI 更多是一个营销术语，而不是科学术语，作为科学家而非技术专家来说。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is my north star, is my field's north star, and I'm happy people call it whatever name they want to call it.
zh: AI 是我的北极星，是我领域的北极星，我很高兴人们想怎么称呼它就怎么称呼。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me ask you maybe this way, like you described, there's kind of these components that from ImageNet and AlexNet took us to where we're today, GPUs essentially, data, label data, just like the algorithm of the model.
zh: 所以让我也许这样问你，正如你描述的，有一些组件，从 ImageNet 和 AlexNet 开始，带我们到了今天，基本上是 GPU，数据，标签数据，还有模型的算法。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's also just the transformer feels like an important step in that trajectory.
zh: 还有 transformer 感觉也是那个轨迹中的重要一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like those are the same components that'll get us to, I don't know, 10 times smarter model, something that's like life-changing for the entire world?
zh: 你觉得这些相同的组件会让我们达到，我不知道，更聪明 10 倍的模型，某种对全世界来说改变生活的东西吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you think we need more breakthroughs?
zh: 还是认为我们需要更多的突破？

speaker: Unknown
en: I know we're going to talk about world models, which I think is a component of this, but is there anything else that you think is like, oh, this will plateau, or okay, this will take us just need more data, more compute, more GPUs?
zh: 我知道我们要谈论世界模型，我认为这是其中的一个组成部分，但还有其他你觉得像，噢，这会达到平台期，或者好的，这只需要更多的数据，更多的算力，更多的 GPU 就能带我们到那里吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh no, I definitely think we need more innovations.
zh: 噢不，我绝对认为我们需要更多的创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think scaling loss of more data, more GPUs, and bigger current model architecture is there's still a lot to be done there, but I absolutely think we need to innovate more.
zh: 我认为缩放，更多的数据，更多的 GPU，更大的当前模型架构，这方面还有很多要做，但我绝对认为我们需要更多的创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not a single deeply scientific discipline in human history that has arrived at a place that says we're done, we're done innovating and AI is one of the, if not the youngest discipline in human civilization in terms of science and technology, we're still scratching the surface.
zh: 人类历史上没有哪一个深刻的科学学科到达了一个说我们完成了、我们不再创新的境地，而人工智能是——如果说不是人类文明中——最年轻的学科之一，就科学和技术而言，我们还在挠皮毛/只是入门。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, like I said, we're going to segue into world models.
zh: 例如，就像我说的，我们要切入到世界模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, you take a model and run it through a video of a couple of office rooms and ask the model to count the number of chairs.
zh: 今天，你拿一个模型，让它跑过几个办公室房间的视频，并要求模型计算椅子的数量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is something a toddler could do or maybe an elementary school kid could do, and AI could not do that, right?
zh: 这是一个蹒跚学步的小孩就能做到的事，或者也许一个小学生就能做到的事，而人工智能做不到那个，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's just so much AI today could not do, then let alone thinking about how did someone like Isaac Newton look at the movements of the celestial bodies and derive an equation or a set of equations that governs the movement of all bodies, that level of creativity, extrapolation, abstraction.
zh: 所以现在有太多人工智能做不到的事，更别提思考像艾萨克·牛顿这样的人是如何观察天体的运动并推导出一个方程或一组支配所有物体运动的方程，那种水平的创造力，外推，抽象。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have no way of enabling AI to do that today.
zh: 我们今天没有办法让人工智能做到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then let's look at emotional intelligence.
zh: 然后让我们看看情感智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look at a student coming to a teacher's office and have a conversation about motivation, passion, what to learn, what's the problem that's really bothering you.
zh: 如果你看一个学生来到老师的办公室并就动力、热情、该学什么、什么问题真正困扰你进行对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: That conversation, as powerful as today's conversational bots are, you don't get that level of emotional cognitive intelligence from today's AI.
zh: 那种对话，尽管今天的对话机器人如此强大，你无法从今天的人工智能那里获得那种水平的情感认知智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a lot we can do better, and I do not believe we're done innovating.
zh: 所以我们有很多可以做得更好的地方，我不相信我们已经完成了创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: Demis had this really interesting interview recently from DeepMind slash Google where someone asked him just like, "What do you think, how far are we from AGI?
zh: Demis 最近有一个非常有趣的采访，来自 DeepMind/Google，有人问他就像，“你怎么想，我们离 AGI 有多远？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does it look like going through there?"
zh: 那个过程看起来是什么样的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: He had a really interesting way of approaching it is if we were to give the most cutting-edge model all the information until the end of the 20th century, see if it could come up with all the breakthroughs Einstein had and so far we're nowhere near that, but they could just-
zh: 他有一个非常有趣的方法来处理这个问题，就是如果我们给最尖端的模型提供直到 20 世纪末的所有信息，看看它是否能提出爱因斯坦拥有的所有突破，到目前为止我们离那个还很远，但他们可以只是——

speaker: Unknown
en: No, we're not.
zh: 不，我们不。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, it's even worse.
zh: 事实上，甚至更糟。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's give AI all the data including modern instruments data of celestial bodies, which Newton did not have, and give it to that and just ask AI to create the 17th century set of equations on the laws of bodily movements.
zh: 让我们给人工智能所有的数据，包括牛顿没有的天体的现代仪器数据，把它给它，并只是让人工智能创建 17 世纪的那套物体运动定律方程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's AI cannot do that.
zh: 今天的人工智能做不到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: All right.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're ways away is what I'm hearing.
zh: 我们还有很长的路要走，这就是我听到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so let's talk about world models.
zh: 好的，所以让我们谈谈世界模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: To me, this is just another really amazing example of you being ahead of where people end up.
zh: 对我来说，这又是一个你领先于人们最终到达的位置的非常令人惊叹的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you were way ahead on, okay, we just need a lot of clean data for AI and neural networks to learn.
zh: 所以你在……遥遥领先，好吧，我们需要大量干净的数据让人工智能和神经网络学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've been talking about this idea of world models for a long time.
zh: 你很长一段时间以来一直在谈论世界模型这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: You started a company to build, essentially there's language models.
zh: 你创办了一家公司来构建，本质上存在语言模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a different thing.
zh: 这是一个不同的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a world model.
zh: 这是一个世界模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll talk about what that is.
zh: 我们会谈论那是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now, as I was preparing for this Elon's talking about world models, Jensen's talking about world models, I know Google's working on this stuff.
zh: 现在，当我为此做准备时，Elon 在谈论世界模型，Jensen 在谈论世界模型，我知道 Google 正在研究这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've been at this for a long time and you actually just launched something that's going, we're going to talk about right before this podcast airs.
zh: 你在这个领域已经很久了，而且你实际上刚刚推出了一个东西，我们要在这个播客发布前正好谈谈它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what is a world model?
zh: 谈谈什么是世界模型？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why is it so important?
zh: 为什么它如此重要？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm very excited to see that more and more people are talking about world models like Elon, like Jensen.
zh: 我很兴奋地看到越来越多的人像 Elon，像 Jensen，在谈论世界模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have been thinking about really how to push AI forward all my life and the large language models that came out of the research world and then OpenAI and all this, for the past few years, were extremely inspiring even for a researcher like me.
zh: 我一生都在思考如何真正推动人工智能前进，那些来自研究界的大型语言模型，然后是 OpenAI 以及所有这些，在过去几年里，即使对像我这样的研究人员来说也非常具有启发性。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remembered when GPT2 came out, and that was in, I think, late 2020.
zh: 我记得当 GPT2 出来时，那是在，我想，2020 年末。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was co-director, I still am, but I was at that time full-time co-director of Stanford's Human-Centered AI institute, and I remember it was... The public was not aware of the power of the large language model yet, but as researchers, we were seeing it, we're seeing the future, and I had pretty long conversations with my natural language processing colleagues like Percy Liang and Chris Manning.
zh: 我是联合主任，我现在还是，但我那时是斯坦福以人为本人工智能研究所的全职联合主任，我记得那是……公众还没有意识到大型语言模型的力量，但作为研究人员，我们在看到它，我们在看到未来，我和我的自然语言处理同事像 Percy Liang 和 Chris Manning 进行了相当长的对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were talking about how critical this technology is going to be and the Stanford AI Institute, Human-Centered AI Institute, HAI, was the first one to establish a full research center foundation model.
zh: 我们在谈论这项技术将变得多么关键，以及斯坦福 AI 研究所，以人为本人工智能研究所，HAI，是第一个建立完整研究中心基础模型的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were, Percy Liang, and many researchers led the first academic paper foundation model.
zh: 我们，Percy Liang，和许多研究人员领导了第一篇学术论文基础模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it was just very inspiring for me.
zh: 所以这对我来说非常具有启发性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, I come from the world of visual intelligence and I was just thinking there's so much we can push forward beyond language because humans, humans use our sense of spatial intelligence, a world understanding to do so many things and they are beyond language.
zh: 当然，我来自视觉智能的世界，我只是在想我们可以超越语言推动很多事情，因为人类，人类使用我们的空间智能感，世界理解来做很多事情，并且它们超越了语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about a very chaotic first responder scene, whether it's fire or some traffic accident or some natural disaster.
zh: 想象一个非常混乱的第一响应者现场，无论是火灾还是一些交通事故或一些自然灾害。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you immerse yourself in those scene and think about how people organize themselves to rescue people, to stop further disasters, to put down fires, a lot of that is movements is spontaneous understanding of objects, worlds, human situational awareness.
zh: 如果你让自己沉浸在那些场景中并思考人们如何组织自己去救人，去阻止进一步的灾难，去扑灭火灾，其中大部分是运动，是物体、世界、人类态势感知的自发理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: Language is part of that, but a lot of those situations, language cannot get you to put down the fire.
zh: 语言是其中的一部分，但在很多这些情况下，语言无法让你扑灭火灾。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that is, what is that?
zh: 所以那就是，那是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I was thinking a lot.
zh: 我在想很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in the meantime, I was doing a lot of robotics research and it dawned on me that the linchpin of connecting the additional intelligence, in addition to language embodied AI, which are robotics, connecting visual intelligence, is the sense of spatial intelligence about understanding the world.
zh: 同时，我在做很多机器人研究，我意识到连接额外智能的关键，除了语言化身的 AI，也就是机器人，连接视觉智能，是关于理解世界的空间智能感。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's when I think it was 2024, I gave a TED talk about spatial intelligence at world models.
zh: 那就是我想是 2024 年，我做了一个关于空间智能在世界模型的 TED 演讲。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I start formulating this idea back in 2022 based on my robotics and computer vision research.
zh: 我在 2022 年开始基于我的机器人和计算机视觉研究制定这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then one thing that was really clear to me is that I really want to work with the brightest technologists and move as fast as possible to bring this technology to life.
zh: 然后有一件事对我来说非常清楚，就是我真的想和最聪明的技术人员合作，并尽可能快地把这项技术带到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's when we founded this company called World Labs.
zh: 那就是我们要创立这家叫 World Labs 的公司的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can see the word world is in the title of our company because we believe so much in world modeling and spatial intelligence.
zh: 你可以看到“World”这个词就在我们公司的标题里，因为我们非常相信世界建模和空间智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are so used to just chatbots and that's a large language model.
zh: 人们太习惯于只有聊天机器人了，而那就是大语言模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: A simple way to understand a world model is you basically describe a scene and it generates an infinitely explorable world.
zh: 理解世界模型的一个简单方法是，你基本上描述一个场景，它就会生成一个无限可探索的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll link to the thing you launched, which we'll talk about, but just is that a simple way to understand it?
zh: 我们会链接到你发布的东西，我们待会儿会聊，但这只是一个理解它的简单方法吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's part of it, Lenny.
zh: 那只是它的一部分，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a simple way to understand a world model is that this model can allow anyone to create any worlds in their mind's eye by prompting whether it's an image or a sentence.
zh: 我认为理解世界模型的一个简单方法是，这个模型可以让任何人通过提示词，无论是图像还是句子，在他们脑海中的眼中创造任何世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also be able to interact in this world whether you are browsing and walking or picking objects up or changing things as well as to reason within this world, for example, if the person consuming, if the agent consuming this output of the world model is a robot, it should be able to plan its path and help to tidy the kitchen, for example.
zh: 并且还能在这个世界中互动，无论你是浏览和行走，还是捡起物体或改变东西，以及在这个世界中进行推理，例如，如果使用这个世界模型输出的智能体是一个机器人，它应该能够规划它的路径并帮助整理厨房，例如。

speaker: Unknown
en: So world model is a foundation that you can use to reason, to interact, and to create worlds.
zh: 所以世界模型是一个你可以用来推理、互动和创造世界的基础。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So robots feels like that's potentially the next big focus for AI researchers and just the impact on the world.
zh: 所以机器人感觉像是 AI 研究人员的下一个大焦点，以及对世界的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what you're saying here is this is a key missing piece of making robots actually work in the real world, understanding how the world works.
zh: 你在这里要说的是，这是让机器人在现实世界中实际工作的一个关键缺失部分，理解世界是如何运作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, first of all, I do think there's more than robots.
zh: 嗯，首先，我确实认为不止是机器人。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's exciting.
zh: 那很令人兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I agree with everything you just said.
zh: 但我同意你刚才说的所有话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think world modeling and spatial intelligence is a key missing piece of embodied AI.
zh: 我认为世界建模和空间智能是具身智能的一个关键缺失部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also think let's not underestimate that humans are embodied agents and humans can be augmented by AI's intelligence.
zh: 我也认为不要低估人类是具身智能体，人类可以被 AI 的智能增强。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like today, humans are language animals, but we're very much augmented by AI helping us to do language tasks including software engineering.
zh: 就像今天，人类是语言动物，但我们在 AI 帮助我们完成语言任务（包括软件工程）方面得到了很大的增强。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that we shouldn't underestimate or maybe we tend not to talk about how humans, as an embodied agents, can actually benefit so much from world models and spatial intelligence models as well as robots can.
zh: 我认为我们不应该低估，或者可能我们倾向于不谈论人类作为一个具身智能体，实际上可以像机器人一样从世界模型和空间智能模型中受益匪浅。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the big unlocks here, robots, which a huge deal if this works out, imagine each of us has robots doing a bunch of stuff for us, they help us with disasters, things like that.
zh: 所以这里的重大解锁，机器人，如果这成功了那就是一件大事，想象我们每个人都有机器人为我们做一堆事情，他们帮助我们要处理灾难之类的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Games obviously is a really cool example, just like infinitely playable games that you just invent out of your head.
zh: 游戏显然是一个非常酷的例子，就像你可以凭空发明出的无限可玩的游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then creativity feels like just like being fun, having fun, being creative, thinking of magic, wild new worlds, and environments.
zh: 然后创造力感觉就像只是为了好玩，找乐子，发挥创造力，想出神奇的、狂野的新世界和环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also design, humans design from machines to buildings to homes and also scientific discovery.
zh: 还有设计，人类从机器到建筑物再到家园的设计，以及科学发现。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is so much.
zh: 有太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to use the example of the discovery of the structure of DNA.
zh: 我喜欢用 DNA 结构发现的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look at one of the most important piece in DNA's discovery history is the x-ray diffraction photo that was captured by Rosalind Franklin, and it was a flat 2D photo of a structure that it looks like a cross with diffractions.
zh: 如果你看看 DNA 发现历史上最重要的部分之一，是 Rosalind Franklin 拍摄的 X 射线衍射照片，那是一张结构的平面的 2D 照片，看起来像一个带有衍射的十字。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can google those photos.
zh: 你可以谷歌那些照片。

speaker: Unknown
en: But with that 2D flat photo, the humans, especially two important humans, James Watson and Francis Crick, in addition to their other information, was able to reason in 3D space and deduce a highly three-dimensional double helix structure of the DNA.
zh: 但是有了那张 2D 平面照片，人类，特别是两个重要的人类，James Watson 和 Francis Crick，除了他们的其他信息之外，能够在 3D 空间中推理并推断出 DNA 的高度三维的双螺旋结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that structure cannot possibly be 2D.
zh: 而那个结构不可能是 2D 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You cannot think in 2D and deduce that structure.
zh: 你无法在 2D 中思考并推断出那个结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to think in 3D spatial, use the human spatial intelligence.
zh: 你必须用 3D 空间思考，使用人类的空间智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think even in scientific discovery, spatial intelligence or AI-assisted spatial intelligence is critical.
zh: 所以我认为即使在科学发现中，空间智能或 AI 辅助的空间智能也是至关重要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is such an example of, I think it was Chris Dixon that had this line that the next big thing is going to start off feeling like a toy.
zh: 这是一个很好的例子，我认为是 Chris Dixon 有这句话，下一件大事开始时感觉会像个玩具。

speaker: Unknown
en: When ChatGPT just came out, I remember Sam Altman just tweeted it as like, "Here's a cool thing we're playing with, check it out."
zh: 当 ChatGPT 刚出来时，我记得 Sam Altman 只是发推文说，“这里有个我们正在玩的很酷的东西，来看看吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, it's the fastest growing product to all of history, changed the world.
zh: 现在，它是历史上增长最快的产品，改变了世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's oftentimes the things that just look like, okay, this is cool, that it's a fun to play with that end up changing the world most.
zh: 而且通常情况下，那些看起来就像，好的，这很酷，玩起来很有趣的东西最终会最大程度地改变世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Sinch, the customer communications cloud.
zh: 本集由 Sinch 为您带来，即客户通信云。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's the thing about digital customer communications.
zh: 关于数字客户通信的事情是这样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you're sending marketing campaigns, verification codes, or account alerts, you need them to reach users reliably.
zh: 无论你是发送营销活动、验证码还是帐户提醒，你都需要它们可靠地到达用户手中。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's where Sinch comes in.
zh: 这就是 Sinch 的用武之地。

speaker: Unknown
en: Over 150,000 businesses, including 8 of the top 10 largest tech companies globally use Sinch's API to build messaging, email, and calling into their products.
zh: 超过 150,000 家企业，包括全球最大的 10 家科技公司中的 8 家，都使用 Sinch 的 API 将消息传递、电子邮件和通话构建到他们的产品中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's something big happening in messaging that product teams need to know about, Rich Communication Services or RCS.
zh: 而且在消息传递方面有一些大事发生，产品团队需要知道，富通信服务或 RCS。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think of RCS as SMS2.0.
zh: 把 RCS 想象成 SMS 2.0。

speaker: Unknown
en: Instead of getting texts from a random number, your users will see your verified company name and logo without needing to download anything new.
zh: 用户收到的不再是随机号码发来的短信，而是会看到你经过验证的公司名称和徽标，而无需下载任何新东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a more secure and branded experience.
zh: 这是一种更安全且具有品牌体验的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: Plus you get features like interactive carousels and suggested replies.
zh: 此外，你还可以获得交互式轮播和建议回复等功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here's why this matters, US carriers are starting to adopt RCS.
zh: 这就是为什么这很重要，美国运营商开始采用 RCS。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sinch is already helping major brands send RCS messages around the world and they're helping Lenny's podcast listeners get registered first before the rush hits the US market.
zh: Sinch 已经在帮助主要品牌在世界各地发送 RCS 消息，他们正在帮助 Lenny 的播客听众在冲击美国市场之前先注册。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn more and get started at sinch.com/lenny.
zh: 在 sinch.com/lenny 了解更多信息并开始使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's S-I-N-C-H.com/lenny.
zh: 那是 S-I-N-C-H.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I reached out to Ben Horowitz, who loves what you're doing, a big fan of yours.
zh: 我联系了 Ben Horowitz，他很爱你在做的事，是你的超级粉丝。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're investors I believe in...
zh: 他们是我相信的投资者……

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, we've known each other for many years, but yes, right now they're investors of World Labs.
zh: 是的，我们认识很多年了，但是是的，目前他们是 World Labs 的投资者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so I asked him what I should ask you about and he suggested ask you why is the bitter lesson alone not likely to work for robots?
zh: 好的，所以我问他我应该问你什么，他建议问你为什么仅靠苦涩的教训不太可能对机器人有效？

speaker: Unknown
en: So first of all, just explain what the bitter lesson was in the history of AI and then just why that won't get us to where we want to be with robots.
zh: 所以首先，解释一下苦涩的教训在人工智能历史中是什么，然后为什么那无法让我们通过机器人达到我们想要的目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, first of all, there are many bitter lessons, but the bitter lessons everybody refers to is a paper written by Richard Sutton who won the Turing Award recently, and he does a lot of reinforcement learning.
zh: 嗯，首先，有很多苦涩的教训，但大家指的苦涩的教训是 Richard Sutton 最近获得图灵奖写的一篇论文，他做很多强化学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Richard has said, if you look at the history, especially the algorithmic development of AI, it turns out simpler model with a ton of data always win at the end of the day instead of the more complex model with less data.
zh: Richard 说过，如果你看历史，尤其是人工智能的算法发展，结果是有大量数据的更简单模型最终总是胜过数据较少的更复杂模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, that was actually... This paper came years after ImageNet.
zh: 我的意思是，那其实是……这篇论文是在 ImageNet 之后几年才出来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That to me was not bitter; it was a sweet lesson.
zh: 对我来说那并不苦涩；那是一个甜美的教训。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why I built ImageNet because I believe that big data plays that role.
zh: 这就是我构建 ImageNet 的原因，因为我相信大数据扮演那个角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why can't bitter lesson work in robotics alone?
zh: 那么为什么苦涩的教训不能单独在机器人领域起作用？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, first of all, I think we need to give credit to where we are today.
zh: 嗯，首先，我认为我们需要给予我们今天所处的位置以肯定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Robotics is very much in the early days of experimentation.
zh: 机器人技术非常处于实验的早期阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: The research is not nearly as mature as say language models.
zh: 研究远未像语言模型那样成熟。

speaker: Unknown
en: So many people are still experimenting with different algorithms and some of those algorithms are driven by big data.
zh: 很多人仍在实验不同的算法，其中一些算法是由大数据驱动的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I do think big data will continue to play a role in robotics, but what is hard for robotics, there are a couple of things.
zh: 所以我确实认为大数据将继续在机器人领域发挥作用，但机器人面临的困难有几个方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is that it's harder to get data.
zh: 一个是获取数据更难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a lot harder to get data.
zh: 获取数据要难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can say, well, there's web data.
zh: 你可以说，嗯，有网络数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is where the latest robotics research is using web videos.
zh: 这就是最新的机器人研究正在使用网络视频的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think web videos do play a role.
zh: 我认为网络视频确实起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you think about what made language model worth a very... As someone who does computer vision and spatial intelligence and robotics, I'm very jealous of my colleagues in language because they had this perfect setup where their training data are in words, eventually tokens, and then they produce a model that outputs words.
zh: 但如果你想一想是什么让语言模型非常有价值……作为一个做计算机视觉、空间智能和机器人技术的人，我很嫉妒我在语言领域的同事，因为他们拥有这种完美的设置，他们的训练数据是单词，最终是词元，然后他们产出一个输出单词的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have this perfect alignment between what you hope to get, which we call objective function and what your training data looks like.
zh: 所以在你希望得到的东西（我们称为目标函数）和你的训练数据的样子之间有这种完美的对齐。

speaker: Unknown
en: But robotics is different.
zh: 但机器人是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even spatial intelligence is different.
zh: 即使是空间智能也是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You hope to get actions out of robots, but your training data lacks actions in 3D worlds, and that's what robots have to do, right?
zh: 你希望从机器人那里得到动作，但你的训练数据缺乏 3D 世界中的动作，而这正是机器人必须做的，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Actions in 3D worlds.
zh: 3D 世界中的动作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to find different ways to fit a, what do they call, a square in a round hole, that what we have is tons of web videos.
zh: 所以你必须找到不同的方式来把一个，他们叫什么，把方钉钉进圆孔里，也就是我们要拥有的是大量的网络视频。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then we have to start talking about adding supplementing data such as teleoperation data or synthetic data so that the robots are trained with this hypothesis of bitter lesson, which is large amount of data.
zh: 那么我们就得开始谈论添加补充数据，比如遥操作数据或合成数据，以便机器人根据苦涩的教训这一假设（即海量数据）进行训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's still hope because even what we are doing in world modeling will really unlock a lot of this information for robots.
zh: 我认为仍有希望，因为即使我们在世界建模方面所做的工作真的将为机器人解锁大量此类信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think we have to be careful because we're at the early days of this and bitter lesson is still to be tested because we haven't fully figured out the data for.
zh: 但我认为我们必须小心，因为我们处于早期阶段，苦涩的教训仍有待测试，因为我们还没有完全搞清楚用于什么的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another part of the bitter lesson of robotics I think we should be so realistic about is again, compared to language models or even spatial models, robots are physical systems.
zh: 我认为我们应该非常现实的关于机器人苦涩教训的另一个部分是，再次与语言模型甚至空间模型相比，机器人是物理系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: So robots are closer to self-driving cars than a large language model.
zh: 所以机器人比大型语言模型更接近自动驾驶汽车。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's very important to recognize.
zh: 认识到这一点非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means that in order for robots to work, we not only need brains, we also need the physical body.
zh: 这意味着为了让机器人工作，我们不仅需要大脑，我们还需要物理身体。

speaker: Unknown
en: We also need application scenarios.
zh: 我们还需要应用场景。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look at the history of self-driving car, my colleague Sebastian Thrun took Stanford's car to win the first DARPA challenge in 2006 or 2005.
zh: 如果你看看自动驾驶汽车的历史，我的同事 Sebastian Thrun 在 2006 年或 2005 年带领斯坦福的车赢得了第一届 DARPA 挑战赛。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's 20 years since that prototype of a self-driving car being able to drive 130 miles in the Nevada desert to today's Waymo and on the street of San Francisco.
zh: 从那辆自动驾驶汽车的原型能够在内华达沙漠行驶 130 英里到今天的 Waymo 和在旧金山街头，已经 20 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're not even done yet.
zh: 而我们甚至还没完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's still a lot.
zh: 还有很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's a 20-year journey.
zh: 所以那是一段 20 年的旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And self-driving cars are much simpler robots, they're just metal boxes running on 2D surfaces, and the goal is not to touch anything.
zh: 自动驾驶汽车是简单得多的机器人，它们只是在 2D 表面上运行的金属盒子，目标是不接触任何东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Robot is 3D things running in 3D world, and the goal is to touch things.
zh: 机器人是在 3D 世界中运行的 3D 事物，目标是接触东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the journey is going to be, there's many aspects, elements, and of course one could say, well, the self-driving car, early algorithm were pre deep learning era.
zh: 所以这段旅程将会是，有很多方面，元素，当然可以说，嗯，自动驾驶汽车，早期算法是前深度学习时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: So deep learning is accelerating the brains.
zh: 所以深度学习正在加速大脑。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's true.
zh: 我认为这是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why I'm in robotics, that's why I'm in spatial intelligence and I'm excited by it.
zh: 这就是我在机器人领域的原因，这就是我在空间智能领域的原因，我对此感到兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: But in the meantime, the car industry is very mature and productizing also involves the mature use cases, supply chains, the hardware.
zh: 但在此期间，汽车行业非常成熟，产品化还涉及成熟的用例、供应链、硬件。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's a very interesting time to work in these problems.
zh: 所以我认为这是一个从事这些问题的非常有趣的时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's true, Ben is right.
zh: 但确实，Ben 是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We might still be subject to a number of bitter lessons.
zh: 我们可能仍然会受到许多苦涩教训的支配。

speaker: Unknown
en: Doing this work, do you ever just feel awe for the way the brain works and is able to do all of this for us?
zh: 做这项工作时，你是否会对大脑的工作方式以及能够为我们做这一切感到敬畏？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just the complexity just to get a machine to just walk around and not hit things and fall, does just give you more respect for what we've already got?
zh: 仅仅是为了让机器能够四处走动而不撞到东西和跌倒的复杂性，确实让我们对我们已经拥有的东西更加尊重？

speaker: Unknown
en: Totally.
zh: 完全是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We operate on about 20 watts.
zh: 我们的运行功率大约是 20 瓦。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's dimmer than any light bulb in the room I'm in right now.
zh: 这比我现在所在房间的任何灯泡都要暗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet we can do so much.
zh: 然而我们可以做这么多事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think actually the more I work in AI, the more I respect humans.
zh: 所以我认为实际上我在 AI 领域工作得越多，我就越尊重人类。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about this product you just launched.
zh: 让我们谈谈你刚刚推出的这款产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's called Marble, a very cute name.
zh: 它叫 Marble，一个很可爱的名字。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what this is, why this is important.
zh: 谈谈这是什么，为什么这很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been playing with it, it's incredible.
zh: 我一直在玩它，它太不可思议了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll link to it for folks to check it out.
zh: 我们会提供链接供大家查看。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is Marble?
zh: 什么是 Marble？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I'm very excited.
zh: 是的，我非常兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: So first of all, Marble is one of the first product that World Labs has rolled out.
zh: 首先是 Marble 是 World Labs 推出的首款产品之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: World Labs is a foundation frontier model company.
zh: World Labs 是一家基础前沿模型公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are founded by four co-founders who have deep technical history.
zh: 我们由四位拥有深厚技术背景的联合创始人创立。

speaker: Unknown
en: My co-founders, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall.
zh: 我的联合创始人，Justin Johnson、Christoph Lassner 和 Ben Mildenhall。

speaker: Unknown
en: We all come from the research field of AI, computer graphics, computer vision, and we believe that spatial intelligence and world modeling is as important, if not more, to language models and complementary to language models.
zh: 我们都来自 AI、计算机图形学、计算机视觉的研究领域，我们认为空间智能和世界建模与语言模型同样重要，甚至更重要，并且是语言模型的补充。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we wanted to seize this opportunity to create deep tech research lab that can connect the dots between frontier models with products.
zh: 所以我们想抓住这个机会，创建一个深度技术研究实验室，可以将前沿模型与产品连接起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Marble is an app that's built upon our frontier models.
zh: 所以 Marble 是一个建立在我们前沿模型之上的应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've spent a year and plus building the world's first generative model that can output genuinely 3D worlds.
zh: 我们花了一年多的时间建立了世界上第一个可以输出真正 3D 世界的生成模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a very, very hard problem.
zh: 这是一个非常非常难的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was a very hard process and we have a team of incredible, founding team of incredible technologists from incredible teams.
zh: 这是一个非常艰难的过程，我们有一个由来自令人难以置信的团队的惊人技术人员组成的创始团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then around just a month or two ago, we saw the first time that we can just prompt with a sentence and the image and multiple images and create worlds that we can just navigate in.
zh: 然后大约就在一两个月前，我们第一次看到我们可以只用一个句子和图像以及多张图像进行提示，并创建我们可以直接导航的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you put it on Google, which we have an option to let you do that, you can even walk around.
zh: 如果你把它放在 Google 上，我们有一个选项让你这样做，你甚至可以四处走动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even though we've been building this for quite a while, it was still just awe-inspiring and we wanted to get into the hands of people who need it.
zh: 尽管我们已经构建了很长一段时间，但它仍然令人敬畏，我们想把它交到需要它的人手中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we know that so many creators, designers, people who are thinking about robotic simulation, people who are thinking about different use cases of navigable interactable, immersive worlds game developers will find this useful.
zh: 然后我们知道有这么多创作者、设计师、那些正在思考机器人仿真的人、那些正在思考可导航、可交互、沉浸式世界的不同用例的人、游戏开发者会发现这很有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we developed Marble as a first step.
zh: 所以我们开发了 Marble 作为第一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's again, still very early, but it's the world's first model doing this, and it's the world's first product that allows people to just prompt, we call it prompt to worlds.
zh: 同样，它还处于非常早期的阶段，但它是世界上第一个这样做的模型，也是世界上第一个允许人们只是提示的产品，我们称之为提示生成世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I've been playing around with it.
zh: 嗯，我一直在玩它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is insane.
zh: 这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could just have a little Shire world where you just infinitely walk around middle earth basically, and there's no one there yet, but it's insane.
zh: 你可以拥有一个小小的夏尔世界，你基本上可以在中土无限地四处走动，虽然那里还没有人，但这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just go anywhere.
zh: 你可以去任何地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's dystopian world.
zh: 有反乌托邦世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just looking at all these examples and my favorite part, actually, I don't know if there's a feature or bug, you can see the dots of the world before it actually renders with all the textures.
zh: 我只是在看所有这些例子，其实我最喜欢的部分，我不知道这是功能还是 bug，你可以在世界实际渲染所有纹理之前看到世界的点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just love like, you get a glimpse into what is going on with this model, basically-
zh: 我只是喜欢，你可以瞥见这个模型基本上正在发生什么——

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so cool to hear because this is where, as a researcher, I am learning because the dots that lead you into the world was an intentional feature visualization, is not part of the model.
zh: 听到这个太酷了，因为作为一名研究人员，我正在学习，因为带你进入世界的点是一个有意的功能可视化，不是模型的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: The model actually just generates the world.
zh: 模型实际上只是生成世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we were trying to find a way to guide people into the world, and a number of engineers worked on different versions, but we converged on the dot, and so many people, you're not the only one, told us how delightful that experience is, and it was really satisfying for us to hear that this intentional visualization feature that's not just the big hardcore model actually has delighted our users.
zh: 但我们试图找到一种方法来引导人们进入世界，许多工程师尝试了不同的版本，但我们最终选择了点，而且很多人，你并不是唯一一个，告诉我们这种体验是多么令人愉快，听到这个有意的可视化功能，不仅仅是那个大型硬核模型，实际上让我们的用户感到高兴，这真的让我们很满意。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you add that to make it more, like to have humans understand what's going on-
zh: 所以你添加它是为了让它更像，为了让人类理解正在发生的事情——

speaker: Dr. Fei-Fei Li
en: To have fun, yes.
zh: 为了好玩，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... get more delightful.
zh: ……变得更令人愉快。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow, that is hilarious.
zh: 哇，这太搞笑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It makes me think about LLMs and the way they, it's not the same thing, but they talk about what they're thinking and what they're doing.
zh: 这让我想到了 LLM 以及它们的方式，这不是同一件事，但它们谈论它们的思考和它们正在做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, it is.
zh: 是的，它是。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It also makes me think about just the Matrix.
zh: 这也让我想起了黑客帝国。

en: It's exactly the Matrix experience.

en: Okay, so just for folks that may want to play around with this, maybe like, what are some applications today that folks can start using today?

zh: 你们这次发布的目标是什么？

speaker: Dr. Fei-Fei Li
en: Yeah, so we do believe that world modeling is very horizontal, but we're already seeing some really exciting use cases, virtual production for movies, because what they need are 3D worlds that they can align with the camera. So when the actors are acting on it, they can position the camera and shoot the segments really well. And we're already seeing incredible use. In fact, I don't know if you have seen our launch video showing Marble. It was produced by a virtual production company. We collaborated with Sony and they use Marble scenes to shoot those videos. So we were collaborating with those technical artists and directors, and they were saying, this has cut our production time by 40X. In fact, it has to-
zh: 是的，所以我们确实相信世界建模是非常通用的，但我们已经看到了一些非常令人兴奋的用例，电影的虚拟制作，因为他们需要的是可以与摄像机对齐的 3D 世界。所以当演员在上面表演时，他们可以定位摄像机并很好地拍摄片段。而且我们已经看到了令人难以置信的使用。实际上，我不知道你是否有看过我们的发布视频展示了 Marble。它是由一家虚拟制作公司制作的。我们与索尼合作，他们使用 Marble 场景来拍摄那些视频。所以我们与那些技术艺术家和导演合作，他们当时说，这将我们的制作时间缩短了 40 倍。事实上，它不得不-

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, in fact it has to, because we only had one month to work on this project and there were so many things they were trying to shoot.
zh: 是的，事实上不得不这样，因为我们只有一个月的时间在这个项目上工作，而他们试图拍摄的东西太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So using Marble really, really significantly accelerated the virtual production for VFX and movies.
zh: 所以使用 Marble 真的、非常显著地加速了 VFX 和电影的虚拟制作。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's one use cases.
zh: 这是一个用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are already seeing our users taking our Marble scene and taking the mesh export and putting games, whether it's games on VR or just fun games that they have developed.
zh: 我们已经看到我们的用户拿走我们的 Marble 场景并导出网格并制作游戏，无论是 VR 上的游戏还是他们开发的一些有趣的游​​戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are showing an example of robotic simulation because when I was, I mean I still am a researcher doing robotic training.
zh: 我们正在展示机器人仿真的例子，因为当我，我的意思是我仍然是一名做机器人训练的研究人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the biggest pain point is to create synthetic data for training robots.
zh: 最大的痛点之一是为机器人创建合成数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this synthetic data needs to be very diverse.
zh: 这种合成数据需要非常多样化。

speaker: Unknown
en: They need to come from different environments with different objects to manipulate.
zh: 它们需要来自具有不同操作对象的不同环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one path to it is to ask computers to simulate.
zh: 获得它的一条途径是让计算机进行仿真。

speaker: Unknown
en: Otherwise, humans have to build every single asset for robots.
zh: 否则，人类必须为机器人构建每一个资产。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's just going to take a lot longer.
zh: 那将花费更长的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we already have researchers reaching out and wanting to use Marble to create those synthetic environments.
zh: 所以我们已经有意向联系的研究人员想要使用 Marble 来创建那些合成环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: We also have unexpected user outreach in terms of how they want to use Marble.
zh: 我们也有意想不到的用户联系我们关于他们想如何使用 Marble。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, a psychologist team called us to use Marble to do psychology research.
zh: 例如，一个心理​​学家团队打电话给我们使用 Marble 进行心理​​学研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: It turned out some of the psychiatric patients they study, they need to understand how their brain respond to different immersive things of different features.
zh: 结果发现他们研究的一些精神病患者，他们需要了解他们的大脑如何对不同特征的不同沉浸式事物做出反应。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, messy scenes or clean scenes or whatever you name it.
zh: 例如，凌乱的场面或干净的场面或任何你能想到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's very hard for researchers to get their hands on these kind of immersive scenes and it will take them too long and too much budget to create.
zh: 研究人员很难获得这种沉浸式场景，创建它们将花费太长时间和太多预算。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Marble is a really almost instantaneous way of getting so many of these experimental environments into their hands.
zh: 而 Marble 真的是一种几乎即时地将这么多实验环境交到他们手中的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're seeing multiple use cases at this point.
zh: 所以我们在这一点上看到了多个用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the VFX, the game developers, the simulation developers as well as designers are very excited.
zh: 但是 VFX、游戏开发人员、仿真开发人员以及设计师都非常兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is very much the way things work in AI.
zh: 这非常像 AI 的工作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've had other AI leaders on the podcast and it's always put things out there early as soon as you can to discover where the big use cases are.
zh: 我请过其他 AI 领导者来播客，总是要尽早把东西发布出去，以发现大的用例在哪里。

speaker: Unknown
en: The head of ChatGPT told me how, when they first put out ChatGPT, he was just scanning TikTok to see how people were using it and all the things they were talking about, and that's what convinced them where to lean in and help them see how people actually want to use it.
zh: ChatGPT 的负责人告诉我，当他们最初发布 ChatGPT 时，他只是在浏览 TikTok 以查看人们如何使用它以及他们谈论的所有事情，这就是让他们确信在哪里投入精力并帮助他们了解人们实际想要如何使用它的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this last use case for therapy.
zh: 我喜欢这最后一个用于治疗的用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just imagining heights, people dealing with heights or snakes or spiders, which-
zh: 我只是在想象高度，人们应对高度或蛇或蜘蛛，这-

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing.
zh: 太神奇了。

speaker: Unknown
en: A friend of mine last night literally called me and talked about his height scare and asked me if Marble should be used.
zh: 我的一位朋友昨晚真的打电话给我，谈论了他的恐高症，并问我是否应该使用 Marble。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing you went straight there.
zh: 你直接想到那真是太神奇了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because imagining all the exposure therapy stuff, this could be so good for that.
zh: 因为想象所有的暴露疗法相关的东西，这可能对此非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so cool.
zh: 这太酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so I should have asked you this before, but I think there's going to be a question of just, how does this differ from things like VO3 and other video generation models?
zh: 好的，所以我之前应该问你这个问题，但我认为会有一个问题，就是这与 VO3 和其他视频生成模型有什么不同？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's pretty clear to me, but I think it might be helpful just to explain how this is different from all the video AI tools people have seen.
zh: 这对我来说很清楚，但我认为解释这与人们见过的所有视频 AI 工具有何不同可能是有帮助的。

speaker: Unknown
en: World Labs' thesis is that spatial intelligence is fundamentally very important, and spatial intelligence is not just about videos.
zh: World Labs 的论点是空间智能基本上非常重要，而空间智能不仅仅关于视频。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, the world is not passively watching videos passing by.
zh: 事实上，世界不是被动地看着视频流逝。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love, Plato has the allegory of the cave analogy to describe vision.
zh: 我喜欢，柏拉图有洞穴寓言的类比来描述视觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: He said that imagine a prisoner tied on his chair, not very humane, but in a cave watching a full life theater in front of him, but the actual life theater that actors are acting is behind his back.
zh: 他说想象一个囚犯被绑在他的椅子上，不是很人道，但在一个山洞里看着面前的一整出戏，但演员实际表演的戏剧在他的身后。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was just lit so that the projection of the action is on a wall of the cave.
zh: 只是点亮了，动作的投影就在洞穴的墙上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the goal, the task of this prisoner is to figure out what's going on.
zh: 然后目标，这个囚犯的任务是弄清楚发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a pretty extreme example, but it really shows, it describes what vision is about, is that to make sense of the 3D world or 4D world out of 2D.
zh: 这是一个非常极端的例子，但它确实展示，它描述了视觉是关于什么的，就是从 2D 中理解 3D 或 4D 世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: So spatial intelligence to me is deeper than only creating that flat 2D world.
zh: 所以对我来说，空间智能不仅仅是创造那个平面的 2D 世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Spatial intelligence to me is the ability to create, reason, interact, make sense of deeply spatial world, whether it's 2D or 3D or 4D, including dynamics and all that.
zh: 对我来说，空间智能是创造、推理、交互、理解深层空间世界的能力，无论是 2D、3D 还是 4D，包括动力学和所有这一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: So World Lab is focusing on that, and of course the ability to create videos per se could be part of this.
zh: 所以 World Lab 正专注于这一点，当然，创造视频本身的能力也可能是其中的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, just a couple of weeks ago, we rolled out the world's first real time demoable, real-time video generation on a single H100 GPU.
zh: 事实上，就在几周前，我们推出了世界上第一个可在单个 H100 GPU 上进行实时演示的、实时的视频生成。

speaker: Unknown
en: So part of our technology includes that, but I think Marble is very different because we really want creators, designers, developers to have in their hands a model that can give them worlds with 3D structures so they can use it for their work.
zh: 所以我们技术的部分内容包括了那个，但我认为 Marble 是非常不同的，因为我们真的希望创作者、设计师、开发者能够掌握一个可以为他们提供具有 3D 结构的世界的模型，以便他们可以在工作中使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why Marble is so different.
zh: 这就是 Marble 如此不同的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way I see it is it's a platform for a ton of opportunity to do stuff.
zh: 在我看来，它是一个可以做很多事情的充满机遇的平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you described, videos are just like, here's a one-off video that's very fun and cool and you could... And that's it.
zh: 正如你所描述的，视频就像是，这是一个一次性视频，非常有趣和酷，你可以……就这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's it.
zh: 就这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you move on.
zh: 然后你就继续往前走。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, we could in Marble, we could allow people to export in video forms.
zh: 顺便说一句，在 Marble 里，我们可以允许人们以视频形式导出。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you could actually, like you said, you go into a world, so let's say it's a hobbit cave.
zh: 所以你实际上可以，就像你说的，你进入一个世界，比如说它是霍比特人的洞穴。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can actually, especially as a creator, you have such a specific way of moving the camera in a trajectory in the director's mind, and then you can export that from Marble into a video.
zh: 你实际上可以，尤其是作为创作者，你在脑海中导演有一种非常特定的移动摄像机轨迹的方式，然后你可以从 Marble 中将其导出为视频。
