en: What does it take to create something like this?
zh: 创造这样的东西需要什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just how big is the team, how many GPUs you work in?
zh: 团队有多大，你们使用了多少 GPU？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything you can share there.
zh: 你能分享些什么吗。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know how much of this is private information, but just what does it take to create something like this that you've launched here?
zh: 我不知道这其中有多少是隐私信息，但创造一个像你在这里推出的这样的东西，需要什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It takes a lot of brain power.
zh: 这需要大量的脑力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we just talk about 20 watts per brain.
zh: 所以我们只谈论每颗大脑 20 瓦。

speaker: Unknown
en: So from that point of view, it's a small number, but it's actually incredible.
zh: 从那个角度来看，这是一个小数字，但实际上它是不可思议的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's half billion years of evolution to give us those power.
zh: 这是五亿年的进化才赋予我们那些力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have a team of 30-ish people now, and we are predominantly researchers and research engineers, but we also have designers and product.
zh: 我们现在有一个大约 30 人的团队，我们主要是研究人员和研究工程师，但我们也有设计师和产品人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: We actually really believe that we want to create a company that's anchored in the deep tech of spatial intelligence, but we are actually building serious products.
zh: 我们真的相信，我们要打造一家以空间智能的深层技术为基石的公司，但我们实际上是在构建严肃的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have this integration of R&D and productization, and of course, we use a ton of GPUs.
zh: 所以我们有这种研发和产品化的结合，当然，我们使用了大量的 GPU。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the technical thing.
zh: 这就是技术方面的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Happy to hear.
zh: 很高兴听到这个。

speaker: Lenny
en: Well, congrats on the launch. I know this is a huge milestone. I know this took a ton of work.
zh: 嗯，恭喜发布。我知道这是一个巨大的里程碑。我知道这付出了大量的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Lenny
en: So I just want to say congrats to you and your team. Let me talk about your founder journey for a moment. So you're a founder of this company. You started how many years ago? A couple of years ago, two, three years ago?
zh: 所以我只想向你和你的团队表示祝贺。让我聊聊你的创始人之旅。所以你是这家公司的创始人。你是在多少年前开始的？几年前，两三年前？

speaker: Unknown
en: A year ago.
zh: 一年前。

speaker: Unknown
en: A year ago?
zh: 一年前？

speaker: Unknown
en: A year plus.
zh: 一年多一点。

speaker: Lenny
en: A year? Okay. Wow.
zh: 一年？好的。哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably, 18 month, yeah.
zh: 大概，18 个月，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's something you wish you knew before you started this that you wish you could whisper into the ear of Fei-Fei of 18 months ago?
zh: 你希望在开始之前就知道什么，让你可以悄悄告诉 18 个月前的 Fei-Fei？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I continue to wish I know the future of technology.
zh: 嗯，我继续希望我知道技术的未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think actually that's one of our founding advantage is that we see the future earlier in general than most people.
zh: 我认为实际上这是我们的创始优势之一，就是我们通常比大多数人更早看到未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: But still, man, this is so exciting and so amazing that what's unknown and what's coming, but I know the reason you're asking me this question is not about the future of technology.
zh: 但是，伙计，这是如此令人兴奋和神奇，关于未知的和即将到来的事物，但我知道你问我这个问题的原因不是关于技术的未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Furthermore, look, I did not start a company of this scale at 20-year-old.
zh: 此外，看，我没有在 20 岁时创办这种规模的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I started a dry cleaner when I was 19, but that's a little smaller scale.
zh: 我在 19 岁时开过一家干洗店，但那个规模小一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We got to talk about that.
zh: 我们得聊聊那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I founded Google Cloud AI and then I founded an institute at Stanford but those are different beasts.
zh: 然后我创立了 Google Cloud AI，然后在斯坦福创立了一个研究所，但那些是不同的野兽。

speaker: Unknown
en: I did feel I was a little more prepared as a founder of the grinding journey compared to maybe the 20-year-old founders.
zh: 我确实觉得，与 20 岁的创始人相比，作为一名创始人，我对这段磨砺之旅的准备稍微充分一些。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I still, I'm surprised, and it puts me into paranoia sometimes that how intensely competitive AI landscape is from the model, the technology itself, as well as talents.
zh: 但我仍然，我很惊讶，这有时让我变得偏执，AI 竞争格局是如此激烈，无论是从模型、技术本身，还是人才方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I founded the company, we did not have these incredible stories of how much certain talents would cost.
zh: 当我创办这家公司时，我们没有这些关于某些人才要花多少钱的惊人故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So these are things that continue to surprise me and I have to be very alert about.
zh: 所以这些事情继续让我感到惊讶，我必须非常警惕。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the competition you're talking about is the competition for talent, the speed at which just how things are moving.
zh: 所以你谈论的竞争是人才的竞争，是事物发展的速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You mentioned this point that I want to come back to that if you just look over the course of your career, you were at all of the major collections of humans that led to so many of the breakthroughs that are happening today.
zh: 你提到了这一点，我想回到这一点，如果你回顾你的职业生涯历程，你身处所有促成今天如此多突破的人类人才聚集地。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, we talk about ImageNet also just SAIL at Stanford is where a lot of the work happened, Google Cloud, which a lot of the breakthroughs happened.
zh: 显然，我们要谈到 ImageNet，还有 Stanford 的 SAIL，那里也是很多工作发生的地方，Google Cloud，那里也有很多突破发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: What brought you to those places?
zh: 是什么把你带到了那些地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: Like for people looking for how to advance in their career, be at the center of the future, just is there a through line there of just what pulled you from place to place and pulled you into those groups that might be helpful for people to hear?
zh: 对于那些寻求如何在职业生涯中晋升、身处未来中心的人来说，那里是否有一条贯穿始终的主线，是什么把你从一个地方拉到另一个地方，把你拉入那些群体，这可能会对人们有帮助？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is actually a great question, Lenny, because I do think about it, and obviously we talked about it's curiosity and passion that brought me to AI, that is more a scientific north star, right?
zh: 是的，这实际上是一个很好的问题，Lenny，因为我确实思考过这个问题，显然我们谈到了是好奇心和激情把我带到了 AI 领域，那更多的是一个科学上的北极星，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: I did not care if AI was a thing or not, so that was one part.
zh: 我并不在乎 AI 是否是一回事儿，所以那是其中一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: But how did I end up choosing in the particular places I work in, including starting World Labs, is I think I'm very grateful to myself or maybe to my parents' genes.
zh: 但是我是如何最终选择我工作的特定地点的，包括创立 World Labs，我认为我非常感激我自己，或者是感激我父母的基因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm an intellectually very fearless person, and I have to say when I hire young people, I look for that because I think that's a very important quality if one wants to make a difference, is that when you want to make a difference, you have to accept that you're creating something new or you're diving into something new.
zh: 我是一个在智力上非常无畏的人，我必须说当我雇佣年轻人时，我会寻找这一点，因为我认为如果一个人想要做出改变，这是一个非常重要的品质，那就是当你想要做出改变时，你必须接受你正在创造新事物或正在潜入新事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: People haven't done that.
zh: 人们还没有做过那些。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you have that self-awareness, you almost have to allow yourself to be fearless and to be courageous.
zh: 如果你拥有那种自我意识，你几乎必须允许自己变得无畏和勇敢。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when I, for example, came to Stanford, in the world of academia, I was very close to this thing called tenure, which is have the job forever at Princeton.
zh: 所以当我，例如，来到 Stanford 时，在学术界的世界里，我非常接近一种叫做终身教职的东西，也就是在 Princeton 永远拥有这份工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I chose to come to Stanford because... I love Princeton.
zh: 但我选择了来 Stanford，因为……我热爱 Princeton。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's by alma mater.
zh: 它是我的母校。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just at that moment there are people who are so amazing at Stanford and the Silicon Valley ecosystem was so amazing that I was okay to take a risk of restarting my tenure clock.
zh: 就在那一刻，Stanford 有那么多令人惊叹的人，而且 Silicon Valley 的生态系统是如此令人惊叹，以至于我可以承担重新开始计算终身教职年限的风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: Becoming the first female director of SAIL, I was actually relatively speaking a very young faculty at that time, and I wanted to do that because I care about that community.
zh: 成为 SAIL 的第一位女性主任，实际上就相对而言我当时是一位非常年轻的教职员工，我想要这样做是因为我在乎那个社区。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't spend too much time thinking about all the failure cases.
zh: 我没有花太多时间思考所有失败的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, I was very lucky that the more senior faculty supported me, but I just wanted to make a difference.
zh: 显然，我非常幸运的是，资深的教职员工支持了我，但我只是想要做出改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then going to Google was similar.
zh: 然后去 Google 也是类似的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wanted to work with people like Jeff Dean, Jeff Hinton, and all these incredible demists, the incredible people.
zh: 我想要和像 Jeff Dean、Jeff Hinton 以及所有这些令人难以置信的专家、这些令人难以置信的人一起工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: The same with World Labs.
zh: World Labs 也是一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have this passion.
zh: 我有这种激情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I also believe that people with the same mission can do incredible things.
zh: 我也相信拥有相同使命的人可以做出令人难以置信的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's how it guided my through line.
zh: 所以这就是它指引我的主线的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't overthink of all possible things that can go wrong because that's too many.
zh: 我不会过度思考所有可能出错的事情，因为那太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like an important element of this is not focusing on the downside, focusing more on the people, the mission.
zh: 我觉得其中一个重要因素不是专注于下行风险，而是更多地专注于人、使命。

speaker: Unknown
en: What gets you excited, what do you think, the curiosity.
zh: 什么让你兴奋，你怎么想，好奇心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do want to say one thing to all the young talents in AI, the engineers, the researchers out there, because some of you apply to World Labs, I feel very privileged you considered World Labs.
zh: 我确实想对 AI 领域所有的年轻人才、工程师、研究人员说一件事，因为你们有些人申请了 World Labs，我觉得很荣幸你们考虑了 World Labs。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do find many of the young people today think about every single aspect of an equation when they decide on jobs.
zh: 我确实发现今天的许多年轻人在决定工作时会考虑等式中的每一个方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: At some point, maybe that's the way they want to do it, but sometimes I do want to encourage young people to focus on what's important because I find myself constantly in mentoring mode when I talk to job candidates.
zh: 在某种程度上，也许那就是他们想要做的方式，但有时我确实想鼓励年轻人专注于重要的事情，因为当我和求职候选人交谈时，我发现自己经常处于指导模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not necessarily recruiting or not recruiting, but just in mentoring mode when I see an incredible young talent who is over-focusing on every minute dimension and aspect of considering a job, when maybe the most important thing is where's your passion?
zh: 不一定是在招聘或不招聘，只是处于指导模式，当我看到一位令人难以置信的年轻人才过度专注于考虑工作时每一个微小的维度和方面，而也许最重要的是你的激情在哪里？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you align with the mission?
zh: 你是否与使命一致？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you believe and have faith in this team?
zh: 你是否相信并信任这个团队？

speaker: Unknown
en: And just focus on the impact and you can make and the kind of work and team you can work with.
zh: 只是专注于你可以产生的影响以及你可以与之一起工作的工作和团队的类型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's tough.
zh: 是的，这很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's tough for people in the AI space.
zh: 对于 AI 领域的人来说这很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now there's so much, so much at them, so much new, so much happening, so much FOMO.
zh: 现在有太多东西向他们袭来，太多新东西，太多事情在发生，太多 FOMO（错失恐惧）。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's true.
zh: 那是真的。

speaker: Lenny
en: I could see the stress. And so I think that advice is really important. Just like what will actually make you feel fulfilled in what you're doing, not just where's the fastest growing company, where's the... Who's going to win? I don't know. I want to make sure I ask you about the work you're doing today at Stanford, at the HCI. I think it's the-
zh: 我能感受到那种压力。所以我认为这个建议真的很重要。就像实际上什么会让你在所做的事情中感到满足，不仅仅是哪里有增长最快的公司，哪里是……谁会赢？我不知道。我想确保我问问你今天在 Stanford 的 HCI 做的工作。我想它是-

speaker: Unknown
en: HAI.
zh: HAI。

speaker: Unknown
en: HAI, Human-Centered AI Institute.
zh: HAI，Human-Centered AI Institute。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you doing there?
zh: 你在那里做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I know this is a thing you do on the side still.
zh: 我知道这是你仍然在业余做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yes, HAI, Human-Centered AI Institute was co-founded by me and a group of faculty like Professor John Etchemendy, Professor James Landay, Professor Chris Manning back in 2018.
zh: 是的，HAI，Human-Centered AI Institute 是我和一组教职员工共同创立的，比如 John Etchemendy 教授、James Landay 教授、Chris Manning 教授，回到 2018 年。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was actually finishing my last sabbatical at Google and it was a very, very important decision for me because I could have stayed in industry, but my time at Google taught me one thing is AI is going to be a civilization of technology.
zh: 实际上我当时正在结束我在 Google 的最后一个休假年，这对我来说是一个非常非常重要的决定，因为我本来可以留在工业界，但我在 Google 的时间教会了我一件事，那就是 AI 将成为一种文明级的技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it dawned on me how important this is to humanity to the point that I actually wrote a piece in New York Times, that year 2018, to talk about the need for a guiding framework to develop and to apply AI.
zh: 我突然意识到这对人类是多么重要，以至于我实际上在 2018 年为 New York Times 写了一篇文章，谈论需要一个指导框架来开发和应用 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that framework has to be anchored in human benevolence, in human centeredness.
zh: 而且那个框架必须锚定在人类的仁慈、以人为中心上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I felt that Stanford, one of the world's top university in the heart of Silicon Valley that gave birth to important companies from NVIDIA to Google, should be a thought leader to create this human-centered AI framework and to actually embody that in our research education and policy and ecosystem work.
zh: 而且我觉得 Stanford，这所位于 Silicon Valley 心脏地带的世界顶尖大学，孕育了从 NVIDIA 到 Google 等重要的公司，应该成为思想领袖来创造这个以人为中心的 AI 框架，并实际上在我们的研究、教育和政策以及生态系统中体现这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I founded HAI.
zh: 所以我创立了 HAI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fast-forward, after six, seven years, it has become the world's largest AI institute that does human-centered research, education, ecosystem, outreach, and policy impact.
zh: 快进到现在，六七年后，它已经成为世界上最大的 AI 研究所，进行以人为中心的研究、教育、生态系统、外联和政策影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: It involves hundreds of faculty across all eight schools at Stanford, from medicine to education, to sustainability to business, to engineering, to humanities to law.
zh: 这涉及到斯坦福大学所有八个学院的数百名教职员工，从医学到教育，到可持续发展，到商业，到工程，到人文，到法律。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we support researchers, especially at the interdisciplinary area from digital economy, to legal studies, to political science, to discovery of new drugs, to new algorithms to that's beyond transformers.
zh: 我们支持研究人员，特别是在跨学科领域，从数字经济，到法律研究，到政治科学，到新药的发现，到超越 transformer 的新算法。

speaker: Unknown
en: We also actually put a very strong focus on policy because when we started HAI, I realized that Silicon Valley did not talk to Washington DC and or Brussels or other parts of the world.
zh: 实际上，我们也非常重视政策，因为当我们创立 HAI 时，我意识到硅谷不与华盛顿特区或布鲁塞尔或世界其他地方对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And given how important this technology is, we need to bring everybody on board.
zh: 鉴于这项技术如此重要，我们需要让每个人都参与进来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we created multiple programs from congressional bootcamp to AI index report to policy briefing, and we especially participated in policymaking including advocating for a national AI research cloud bill that was passed in the first Trump administration and participating in state level regulatory AI discussions.
zh: 因此，我们创建了从国会训练营到 AI 指数报告再到政策简报的多个项目，我们特别参与了政策制定，包括倡导在第一届特朗普政府期间通过的国家 AI 研究云法案，以及参与州级 AI 监管讨论。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a lot we did, and I continue to be one of the leaders even though I'm much less involved operationally because I care not only we create this technology, but we use it in the right way.
zh: 所以我们做了很多，我也继续成为领导者之一，尽管我在运营方面的参与度大大降低了，因为我不仅关心我们创造这项技术，还关心我们以正确的方式使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was not aware of all that other work you were doing.
zh: 我并不了解你正在做的所有其他工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you're talking, I was reminded Charlie Munger had this quote, "Take a simple idea and take it very seriously."
zh: 当你说话时，我想起了查理·芒格的一句话，“接受一个简单的想法，并且非常认真地对待它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like you've done that in so many different ways and stayed with it and it's unbelievable the impact that you've had in so many ways over the years.
zh: 我觉得你在很多不同的方面都做到了这一点，并坚持了下来，多年来你在许多方面产生的影响力是令人难以置信的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to skip the lightning round and I'm just looking to ask you one last question.
zh: 我要跳过闪电战环节，只想问你最后一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else that you wanted to share?
zh: 你还有什么想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else you want to leave listeners with?
zh: 还有什么想留给听众的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I am very excited by AI, Lenny.
zh: 我对 AI 非常兴奋，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to answer one question that when I travel around the world, everybody asks me is that, if I'm a musician, if I'm a teacher, middle school teacher, if I'm a nurse, if I'm an accountant, if I'm a farmer, do I have a role in AI or is AI just going to take over my life or my work?
zh: 我想回答一个问题，当我在世界各地旅行时，每个人都会问我这个问题，如果我是一名音乐家，如果我是一名教师，中学教师，如果我是一名护士，如果我是一名会计，如果我是一名农民，我在 AI 中有角色吗，还是 AI 只是会接管我的生活或我的工作？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think this is the most important question of AI and I find that in Silicon Valley, we tend not to speak heart-to-heart with people, with people like us and not like us in Silicon Valley, but all of us, we tend to just toss around words like infinite productivity or infinite leisure time or infinite power or whatever.
zh: 我认为这是 AI 最重要的问题，我发现在硅谷，我们往往不与人们进行心连心的交流，与像我们一样的人和不像我们的人在硅谷，但我们所有人，我们倾向于只是随意抛出诸如无限生产力或无限闲暇时间或无限权力之类的词。

speaker: Unknown
en: But at the end of the day, AI is about people.
zh: 但归根结底，AI 关乎的是人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when people ask me that question, it's a resounding yes, everybody has a role in AI.
zh: 当人们问我这个问题时，答案是响亮的肯定，每个人都在 AI 中有角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: It depends on what you do and what you want.
zh: 这取决于你做什么和你想要什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But no technology should take away human dignity and the human dignity and agency should be at the heart of the development, the deployment, as well as the governance of every technology.
zh: 但没有任何技术应该夺走人类尊严，人类尊严和主观能动性应该处于每项技术的开发、部署和治理的核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you are a young artist and your passion is storytelling, embrace AI as a tool.
zh: 所以，如果你是一名年轻的艺术家，你的热情是讲故事，请拥抱 AI 作为一种工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, embrace Marble.
zh: 事实上，拥抱 Marble。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hope it becomes a tool for you because the way you tell your story is unique and the world still needs it.
zh: 我希望它成为你的工具，因为你讲故事的方式是独特的，世界仍然需要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But how you tell your story, how do you use the most incredible tool to tell your story in the most unique way is important.
zh: 但是你如何讲述你的故事，如何使用最不可思议的工具以最独特的方式讲述你的故事，这很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that voice needs to be heard.
zh: 那个声音需要被听到。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you are a farmer near retirement, AI still matters because you are a citizen.
zh: 如果你是一名即将退休的农民，AI 仍然很重要，因为你是一名公民。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can participate in your community, you should have a voice in how AI is used, how AI is applied.
zh: 你可以参与你的社区，你应该对 AI 如何使用、AI 如何应用有发言权。

speaker: Unknown
en: You work with people that you can encourage all of you to use AI to make life easier for you.
zh: 你与你鼓励的所有人一起工作，使用 AI 让你的生活更轻松。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you are a nurse, I hope you know that at least in my career, I have worked so much in healthcare research because I feel our healthcare workers should be greatly augmented and helped by AI technology, whether it's smart cameras to feed more information or robotic assistance because our nurses are overworked, overfatigued, and as our society ages, we need more help for people to be taken care of.
zh: 如果你是一名护士，我希望你知道，至少在我的职业生涯中，我在医疗保健研究方面做了很多工作，因为我觉得我们的医护人员应该得到 AI 技术的大力增强和帮助，无论是提供更多信息的智能摄像头还是机器人辅助，因为我们的护士过度劳累、过度疲劳，而且随着我们的社会老龄化，我们需要更多的帮助来照顾人们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So AI can play that role.
zh: 所以 AI 可以发挥那个作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I just want to say that it's so important that even a technologist like me are sincere about that everybody has a role in AI.
zh: 所以我只想说，即使是像我这样的技术专家，真诚地认为每个人都在 AI 中有角色，这一点非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: What a beautiful way to end it.
zh: 这是一个多么美好的结束方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Such a tie back to where we started about how it's up to us and take individual responsibility for what AI will do in our lives.
zh: 与我们开始的地方如此紧密地联系在一起，关于这取决于我们，并对 AI 在我们生活中的作用承担个人责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Final question, where can folks find Marble?
zh: 最后一个问题，人们在哪里可以找到 Marble？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where can they go, maybe try to join World Labs if they want to?
zh: 他们可以去哪里，也许如果想加入 World Labs 的话？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the website?
zh: 网站是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where do people go?
zh: 人们去哪里？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, World Labs website is www.worldlabs.ai and you can find our research progress there.
zh: 嗯，World Labs 的网站是 www.worldlabs.ai，你可以在那里找到我们的研究进展。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have technical blogs.
zh: 我们有技术博客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find Marble, the product there.
zh: 你可以在那里找到 Marble 这个产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can sign in there.
zh: 你可以在那里登录。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find our job posts link there.
zh: 你可以在那里找到我们的招聘职位链接。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're in San Francisco.
zh: 我们在旧金山。

speaker: Unknown
en: We love to work with the world's best talents.
zh: 我们喜欢与世界上最好的人才一起工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fei-Fei, thank you so much for being here.
zh: 李飞飞，非常感谢你的到来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you, Lenny.
zh: 谢谢你，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bye everyone.
zh: 再见大家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢您的收听。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you found this valuable, you can subscribe to the show on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app.
zh: 如果您觉得这很有价值，您可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或您最喜欢的播客应用上订阅该节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们评分或留下评论，因为这确实能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com.
zh: 您可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到所有往期节目或了解更多关于该节目的信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: See you in the next episode.
zh: 下一集见。
